urn:contentItem:6JK3-7J23-RTTV-W25X-00000-00SC JOHNSON & SON - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:09ZLexisNexisSTOCKSC JOHNSON & SON - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: SC JOHNSON & SON

PermID4298009420Websitehttp://www.scjohnson.com/en/home.aspx?directLink=yIndustryManufacturer of household products

ACTIVITIES:

SC Johnson & Son, founded in 1886 in Racine, Wisconsin, is a family-owned consumer goods company. With $11 billion in revenue and 13,000 employees, it manufactures household brands like Windex, Glade, and Ziploc, operating in 70 countries. SC Johnson emphasizes sustainability, using renewable energy and reducing plastic waste. Its innovation center develops eco-friendly products, and its transparency in ingredient disclosure sets industry standards. Led by H. Fisk Johnson, the companys philanthropy includes land conservation and education grants. SC Johnsons commitment to quality and environmental responsibility reinforces its global leadership in household goods.

+ SC JOHNSON & SON [3 News That Matters]

+ SC JOHNSON EXPANDS OUTDOOR OFFERINGS WITH NEW OFF! AND RAID PRODUCTS

SC Johnson & Son is helping customers prepare for the outdoor season with the launch of several new products under its OFF! and Raid brands. As consumers gear up for spring and summer activities, the company has unveiled a new line of outdoor products designed to keep patios, lawns, and gardens bug-free. This announcement was made on 7th May 2026.The new offerings from SC Johnson in the OFF! insect repellent family include various formulations aimed at controlling mosquitoes, ants, and wasps. These products are tailored for spring hosting, gardening, and enhancing outdoor spaces, making them a timely addition to the market as warmer weather approaches. Prices for these outdoor products start at $5, appealing to a broad range of consumers looking to enjoy their backyards without the nuisance of insects.In addition to the new OFF! and Raid products, SC Johnson is making strategic moves in its acquisition strategy. The company has announced an agreement to acquire the global Space Bag brand from ITW, which is expected to be finalized in the coming months, pending U.S. regulatory approvals. Financial terms of this acquisition have not been disclosed, but SC Johnson is actively developing transition plans for the brand.Further expanding its portfolio, SC Johnson has also acquired Babyganics, a rapidly growing personal care brand focused on family-oriented products. Founded in 2004, Babyganics offers a wide range of items including skin care, bath care, insect repellent, and cleaning products. This acquisition aligns with SC Johnson's strategy to enhance its presence in the family and personal care market, reinforcing its commitment to providing safe and effective products for consumers.As SC Johnson continues to innovate and expand its product lines, the company is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for outdoor and family care products. The introduction of new OFF! and Raid products, along with strategic acquisitions, underscores SC Johnson's commitment to delivering quality solutions that meet the evolving needs of consumers.

+ SC JOHNSON PARTNERS WITH EARTHECHO INTERNATIONAL TO COMBAT PLASTIC WASTE

SC Johnson & Son is continuing its commitment to environmental sustainability through a partnership with EarthEcho International, aimed at combating plastic waste. The initiative marks the third year of collaboration between the two organizations and was announced on the 31st of March, 2026. In this latest phase, the Marine Plastics Ambassadors program will expand its reach by launching its largest cohort yet, empowering young advocates to tackle the pressing issue of plastic pollution affecting ecosystems worldwide.The Marine Plastics Ambassadors program seeks to provide participants with advocacy training and opportunities for policy engagement. Young changemakers will be equipped with essential tools and mentorship, enabling them to devise scalable solutions tailored to their local communities. This approach aims to actively engage participants in the fight against plastic waste and strengthen environmental protections across the nation. Philippe Cousteau, Jr., founder of EarthEcho International, emphasized the importance of empowering youth to drive change, stating, "The Marine Plastics Ambassadors are leading the way, and EarthEcho is proud to build a community of practice that empowers them to change the world."Fisk Johnson, Chairman and CEO of SC Johnson, expressed optimism about the involvement of young people in addressing plastic pollution. He remarked, "It's encouraging to see young people stepping forward to help tackle this important issue. As a company, we advocate for smart government regulation as a crucial tool in tackling the plastic waste crisis. We're pleased to partner with EarthEcho to support this next generation of leaders in their advocacy efforts."Participants in the Marine Plastics Ambassadors program will gather in Washington, D.C., this summer to advance their policy priorities and engage with legislators. The meeting aims to demonstrate the effectiveness of youth-driven solutions in reducing plastic pollution. With over 430 million tons of plastic produced globally each year, SC Johnson’s support for EarthEcho’s initiatives underscores the urgency of addressing plastic waste, particularly as projections indicate that plastic entering oceans could nearly triple by 2040.As environmental concerns mount, the partnership between SC Johnson and EarthEcho International signifies a proactive step towards mobilizing future generations. The program not only provides essential training but also fosters a network of advocates dedicated to creating impactful change. For more information on the Marine Plastics Ambassadors and their initiatives, visit EarthEcho's official website.

+ SC JOHNSON UNVEILS NEW OFF! DEEP WOODS MAX AEROSOL FOR OUTDOOR ENTHUSIASTS

SC Johnson & Son has launched a new product in its OFF! Deep Woods insect repellent line, introducing the Deep Woods MAX aerosol. The new formulation is designed specifically for outdoor users seeking extended protection against insects. On 2nd April 2026, SC Johnson announced that the Deep Woods MAX aerosol is a picaridin-based formula that effectively repels mosquitoes and ticks for up to 10 hours.The OFF! Deep Woods MAX aerosol is marketed as fragrance-free and non-greasy, making it suitable for those engaged in outdoor activities. Additionally, the product is touted as safe to use around common outdoor equipment, including fishing lines, sporting gear, and painted surfaces, which is a significant consideration for outdoor enthusiasts and anglers.This product launch coincides with SC Johnson's new sponsorship arrangement, wherein the OFF! brand becomes the official mosquito and tick repellent of Major League Fishing (MLF) for the year 2026. The partnership will feature brand presence at various MLF events and include a sponsored angler team participating in the four 2026 MLF Team Series events, enhancing SC Johnson's visibility within the fishing community.The collaboration aims to leverage the popularity of fishing as a recreational activity while promoting the benefits of the OFF! Deep Woods MAX aerosol. SC Johnson's strategic approach to align with MLF is expected to boost brand awareness and potentially drive sales among outdoor enthusiasts who prioritize effective insect protection.As the outdoor activity market continues to grow, SC Johnson's introduction of the OFF! Deep Woods MAX aerosol addresses a specific need for effective insect repellents, reinforcing the company's commitment to providing innovative solutions for consumers. This product launch and sponsorship are poised to enhance SC Johnson's position in the competitive insect repellent market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE [3 News That Matters]

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE NAMES NICOLAS TRAN DINH AS NEW APAC DIGITAL & MEDIA DIRECTOR

Colgate-Palmolive Company has announced the appointment of Nicolas Tran Dinh as the new APAC Digital & Media Director. In this role, Dinh will be responsible for spearheading full-funnel media strategies, geographic operations, and digital transformation initiatives across the Asia-Pacific region. This strategic move is expected to enhance Colgate-Palmolive's digital presence and streamline its marketing efforts in one of the fastest-growing markets.The announcement was made on 13th May 2026, highlighting the company's commitment to adapting its marketing and media strategies to meet the evolving demands of consumers in the digital age. With a focus on leveraging data analytics and innovative digital solutions, Dinh aims to strengthen Colgate-Palmolive's brand engagement and drive growth across diverse markets within the APAC region.Dinh brings a wealth of experience in digital marketing and media management to Colgate-Palmolive, having previously held leadership roles in major multinational companies. His expertise in digital transformation will be crucial as the company navigates the complexities of the modern advertising landscape, especially in the wake of recent shifts in consumer behavior and media consumption patterns.Colgate-Palmolive's decision to elevate Dinh underscores its strategic priority of integrating digital technologies into its core operations. The company is keen on optimizing its marketing spend while ensuring that its digital initiatives resonate effectively with the target audience. As digital advertising continues to evolve, the appointment of a dedicated leader in this space signals the company's proactive approach to maintaining its competitive edge.As Dinh steps into this critical role, industry observers will be watching closely to see how Colgate-Palmolive adapts its strategies in response to the ongoing challenges and opportunities in the digital marketplace. With rising competition and the need for innovative marketing solutions, Dinh's leadership could play a pivotal role in shaping the future of Colgate-Palmolive's brand presence in the APAC region.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE LAUNCHES 'EVERY COLGATE SMILE HAS A STORY' CAMPAIGN

MUMBAI, India – Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited is embarking on a new journey with the launch of its storytelling campaign, 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story.' This initiative celebrates its 90-year legacy in India, where the iconic Colgate tube has become an integral part of countless households, particularly among mothers who view it as a trusted member of the family. This announcement comes on 6th May 2026, marking nearly a century of the brand's presence in the country.The campaign is designed to highlight the profound connection that Indian mothers have with Colgate, portraying the universal desire to protect their families. The series delves into the lives of real people, showcasing their stories and the impact of Colgate's products. The first film, 'Raised by Sweets,' introduces viewers to Vertica Khandelwal's family in Dehradun, who have been creating exquisite sweets for 125 years. As she navigates the joys of sharing sweets with her children, her confidence in Colgate's protective benefits serves as a reassuring backdrop.At the heart of the campaign lies the sentiment of a mother's peace of mind, reinforced by Colgate's scientific advancements. The company emphasizes its Arginine Calcium Boost Technology, which is designed to offer 24-hour cavity protection. Gunjit Jain, Executive Vice President – Marketing at Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited, expressed that this campaign goes beyond traditional advertising, reinforcing the trust that mothers have placed in Colgate over the decades.Colgate-Palmolive aims to amplify the 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' series across various platforms including television, digital media, and social channels. The initiative aligns with the company's broader mission to foster a healthier future for people and the planet, showcasing its commitment to sustainability and community well-being. The brand has made significant strides in reducing plastic waste, promoting recyclability, and enhancing oral health education for children through its Colgate Bright Smiles, Bright Futures® program.As it enters this new chapter, Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited continues to focus on innovation and inclusivity, aspiring to create a lasting impact on both consumers and the environment. This storytelling platform not only aims to celebrate its rich heritage but also to connect with the next generation of families, ensuring that the Colgate legacy continues to thrive.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE CELEBRATES 90 YEARS OF TRUST IN INDIA

Colgate-Palmolive Company has marked a significant milestone in India, celebrating 90 years of being a trusted part of Indian households. This iconic brand, known for its oral care products, is now showcasing its legacy through the 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' campaign. Launched on the 6th of May, 2026, the campaign aims to highlight the emotional connections families have with the brand and its products, reinforcing its position as a leader in oral health solutions.The 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' campaign captures real-life narratives, emphasizing the bond between mothers and their children, driven by the instinct to protect. One such story features Vertica Khandelwal from Dehradun, who juggles cultural heritage and the importance of dental health, demonstrating how Colgate provides reassurance with its slogan 'Colgate Sambhal Lega'. This campaign creatively blends emotional storytelling with the scientific advancements that Colgate-Palmolive has pioneered in oral care.Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited continues to lead the oral care market by combining emotional resonance with scientific integrity. The brand's commitment to innovation is evidenced by its Arginine + Calcium Boost Technology, which delivers 24-hour cavity protection. As the number one oral care brand in India, Colgate is dedicated to offering reliable solutions that meet the needs of its consumers while maintaining a focus on sustainable and inclusive growth.This campaign not only emphasizes the brand's heritage but also reflects Colgate-Palmolive's understanding of the importance of trust in consumer relationships. By connecting with families through relatable stories, the brand reinforces its promise of protection and care, which has been a cornerstone of its identity in the Indian market for nearly a century.As Colgate-Palmolive continues to evolve its marketing strategies, the 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' campaign serves as a reminder of the brand's enduring impact on Indian households. By fostering emotional connections and emphasizing their scientific advancements, Colgate-Palmolive aims to strengthen its legacy while ensuring that it remains a trusted partner in oral health for generations to come.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PROCTER AND GAMBLE [3 News That Matters]

+ PROCTER & GAMBLE SECURES TOP SPOT IN HOUSEHOLD PRODUCTS ON FORTUNE'S 2026 INNOVATION LIST

Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) has garnered the number one position in the Household Products Industry on Fortune's 2026 America's Most Innovative Companies list, a title it has maintained for three consecutive years. The announcement was made on 23rd April 2026, emphasizing the company's strength in Product Innovation among a field of 300 leading innovators in the United States.The Fortune ranking, produced in collaboration with Statista, evaluates companies across three critical dimensions: Product Innovation, Process Innovation, and Innovation Culture. This comprehensive assessment ensures that Procter & Gamble's innovative capabilities are recognized in a rigorous manner, placing the company among America's most elite organizations with a combined revenue exceeding $12.5 trillion.Victor Aguilar, Chief Research, Development & Innovation Officer at Procter & Gamble, stated, "This recognition is a testament to our incredible organization around the world and their commitment to pushing the boundaries of what's possible, transforming how we innovate." He emphasized the company's consumer-first approach, focusing on deeply understanding consumer needs and employing advanced science and technology to develop solutions that resonate with customers.Procter & Gamble's sustained recognition in Product Innovation reflects substantial investments across its diverse brand portfolio. The company continues to integrate innovation strategies across various categories, including Fabric Care, Home Care, and Personal Health Care. This approach enables Procter & Gamble to leverage its extensive intellectual property portfolio, translating scientific advancements into products that enhance consumer lives.As an industry leader, Procter & Gamble's commitment to innovation is further demonstrated in its robust performance in the Fortune 500 rankings. The company's diverse range of trusted brands, including Tide®, Gillette®, and Olay®, solidifies its position as a key player in the global market, serving consumers in approximately 70 countries. For more information on Procter & Gamble and its initiatives, please visit https://www.pg.com.

+ P&G PRO REVIVES MR. CLEAN: A NEW ERA FOR PROFESSIONAL CLEANING SOLUTIONS

CINCINNATI--Mr. Clean is back from retirement—and with Mr. Clean PRO, he’s ready for hire as businesses’ most dependable new employee with a full cleaning portfolio to get the job done. Today, 10th April 2026, Mr. Clean PRO is introducing the next generation of professional cleaning to help businesses clean more efficiently and effectively, making him the employee businesses can always count on.Cleaning is a critical aspect of operations across various sectors, including restaurants, retail, and offices. Mr. Clean PRO aims to address frequent challenges faced by professionals, particularly regarding the effectiveness of current surface cleaning solutions. With commercial-grade formulas specifically designed to tackle grease, stains, and grime, Mr. Clean PRO offers businesses a reliable option to enhance their cleaning operations.Bryan Smith, Vice President North America at Procter & Gamble PRO, emphasized the importance of providing solutions that meet the needs of professionals. He stated, 'With Mr. Clean PRO, we’re helping to address those areas of need because choosing the right surface and floor cleaner can make a big difference.' This sentiment reflects a growing demand for effective cleaning solutions that allow businesses to maintain high standards of cleanliness and efficiency.The new Mr. Clean PRO line includes several innovative products tailored for commercial use, such as the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial All Surface Cleaner and the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial Deep Cleaner with Bleach. These products are specifically engineered to deliver the same dependability and results that the Mr. Clean brand is known for in household cleaning, but with enhanced performance for commercial environments.Mr. Clean PRO products are available nationwide through various distributors and retailers, both in stores and online. With sizes ranging from 1-gallon to 32oz, businesses can easily access the cleaning solutions they need to keep operations running smoothly. For more information, visit pgpro.com and follow P&G PRO on social media for updates on future innovations and offerings.

+ P&G EXPANDS BRAND ENGAGEMENT THROUGH WNBA PARTNERSHIP

Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) has announced a multi-year, multi-brand partnership with the Women’s National Basketball Association (WNBA) that aims to enhance brand relevance and engage a broader audience. This strategic collaboration is set to include various flagship P&G brands in athlete collaborations, league events, and digital storytelling campaigns, marking a significant step for the company within the realm of women’s sports.The partnership, revealed on the 9th of April, 2026, is designed to deepen engagement with professional women's basketball and its rapidly growing fan base. As P&G increasingly focuses on brand relevance and emotional connections rather than just product placement on shelves, this initiative aligns with current trends in sports marketing and digital media. By tapping into the rising visibility of women's sports, P&G aims to connect with younger and more diverse consumers, positioning its legacy brands favorably in a competitive marketplace.Looking ahead, the effectiveness of these WNBA campaigns will be closely monitored by investors, particularly in terms of their impact on brand equity and customer loyalty. Procter & Gamble's marketing spend in sports and entertainment will likely be influenced by the execution and scale of these initiatives. Investors are keen to see how this partnership will contribute to the company’s broader strategy to connect with underrepresented demographics, especially as the WNBA continues to gain popularity.Despite the optimistic outlook, there are considerations for investors regarding P&G's financial health and its commitment to disciplined balance sheet management. With the company’s share price currently trading at approximately 13% below analyst expectations, concerns about short-term momentum, including a recent 6.6% decline over the past month, may raise questions about the timing of this marketing investment. Investors will be looking for clear disclosures from Procter & Gamble regarding the returns from this partnership in upcoming earnings reports.In summary, the collaboration between Procter & Gamble and the WNBA represents a strategic move to bolster brand storytelling and visibility among a younger audience. As this partnership unfolds, stakeholders will be keen on how effectively it reinforces P&G's established brands and appeals to a fast-growing market segment while managing the associated financial risks.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RB [3 News That Matters]

+ RECKITT BENCKISER'S DIGITAL SHIFT IN CHINA GAINS INVESTOR ATTENTION

Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC is navigating a pivotal moment as it adapts to China's fast-evolving e-commerce landscape. Kris Licht, CEO of Reckitt, emphasizes the significance of this transition, describing it as "the most profound channel shift" he has encountered. As of 9th May 2026, Reckitt has reported that e-commerce and social commerce have surged to account for roughly 80% of its sales in China, a dramatic increase from 30% six years ago. The company’s revenue from the region is nearing £1 billion, buoyed by 11 consecutive quarters of double-digit growth, as Licht revealed in an interview with Semafor.The timing of Reckitt's strategic pivot comes as the company aims to reassure investors about its digital sales initiatives, accelerated product cycles, and the integration of artificial intelligence. This comes in the wake of a challenging year marked by soft demand for cold-and-flu products, rising costs driven by oil prices, and ongoing struggles in Russia. Reckitt's upcoming "Focus On: Digital Science" virtual investor event on 14th May is expected to showcase how its digital and R&D teams are leveraging technology to foster innovation and drive category growth, providing a timely backdrop for Licht to reshape the narrative around Reckitt's operations in China.Despite these strategic moves, Reckitt's stock performance has faced scrutiny. The company recently disclosed a buyback of 190,500 ordinary shares, totaling about £9 million, as part of its efforts to maintain capital returns. However, there are lingering concerns about Reckitt's financial outlook, particularly after missing first-quarter revenue expectations and forecasting a dip in first-half margins. Analysts like Harsharan Mann from Aviva Investors have characterized the quarter as one of "broad-based muted growth," while JPMorgan's Celine Pannuti has highlighted uncertainty regarding Reckitt's ability to meet full-year targets, particularly in emerging markets.The competitive landscape is further complicated by rising costs affecting the entire consumer staples sector. Companies like Unilever have announced plans to implement modest price increases to counteract inflation, while Reckitt has also signaled potential margin pressures. The shift in consumer preferences towards lower-priced private-label goods raises additional challenges for Reckitt, especially as some shoppers are already exploring alternatives to its branded health and hygiene products. Market analysts, including Zavier Wong from eToro, suggest that companies that have hedged against these pressures are better positioned to weather the storm.Moreover, Reckitt’s operations in Russia remain a point of contention, as new EU sanctions have complicated its product offerings. The company's local arm is actively working on new product development and filing for new intellectual property to fill gaps left by the sanctions. While Licht remains optimistic that accelerated moves could mitigate some of these challenge

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP POSITIONED FOR GROWTH AS AIR FRESHENERS MARKET EXPANDS

The air fresheners market is poised for significant expansion, with projections indicating a rise to $19.75 billion by 2030. This growth is underpinned by urbanization and innovation in fragrance technologies. As of 27th April 2026, the market is expected to grow from $14.36 billion in 2025 to $15.43 billion in 2026, reflecting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.5%. The increasing awareness of indoor ambiance, heightened household spending, and greater retail accessibility are key contributors to this trend.The anticipated growth trajectory of the air fresheners market is bolstered by factors such as a rising preference for enhancing home comfort and a growing demand for products that improve lifestyle quality. Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC, alongside competitors like Godrej Consumer Products and Procter & Gamble, is actively engaging in the development of innovative fragrance solutions. The market's expansion is further fueled by a focus on wellness-driven environments and the enhancement of indoor air quality, as consumers become more health-conscious.Urbanization plays a pivotal role in the increasing demand for air fresheners, as more individuals migrate to urban centers in search of improved living standards. This shift in lifestyle drives the need for indoor fragrance solutions, particularly in densely populated areas. Furthermore, rising air pollution levels, primarily from industrialization and vehicle emissions, have created a heightened demand for air fresheners that can mask unpleasant odors and improve perceived indoor air quality.In response to growing environmental concerns, Reckitt Benckiser Group and other major players in the air fresheners market are innovating with advanced aerosol dispersion technologies. These innovations focus on precise and efficient fragrance delivery while minimizing environmental impacts. For example, Everspray's micro-diffusion aerosol system exemplifies this trend by delivering ultra-fine microdroplets for consistent scent coverage with minimal waste.Regionally, Europe led the air fresheners market in 2025, while the Asia-Pacific region is anticipated to emerge as the fastest-growing market in the coming years. As companies like Reckitt Benckiser Group continue to develop sustainable solutions and adapt to evolving consumer preferences, they are well-positioned to capitalize on the anticipated market growth. Investors are keenly observing these developments as they evaluate future opportunities in this expanding sector.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER'S HARRY KIRSCH JOINS GALDERMA BOARD

LONDON - Reckitt Benckiser Group plc announced that Harry Kirsch, one of its non-executive directors, has been appointed to the board of Galderma (SIX:GALD) as of 28th April 2026. This strategic move is seen as a significant step for both companies, given Galderma's focus on dermatological products and Reckitt's extensive portfolio in consumer health and hygiene.The appointment was disclosed in accordance with Listing Rule 9.6.14 of the Financial Conduct Authority’s Listing Rules, as detailed in a press release from Reckitt Benckiser. While the company has not provided additional information regarding Kirsch’s specific role at Galderma or the terms of his appointment, his experience is expected to bring valuable insights to the Swiss-based firm.Kirsch continues to serve as a non-executive director at Reckitt Benckiser, a leading consumer health and hygiene products company known for its prominent brands including Dettol, Lysol, Nurofen, and Mucinex. His dual roles may foster synergies between Reckitt Benckiser and Galderma, particularly in areas of product innovation and market expansion.Galderma, which operates in the highly competitive skincare and dermatology sector, has been positioning itself for growth, and the addition of Kirsch could signal a commitment to enhancing its governance and strategic direction. Investors will be keen to see how this appointment influences Galderma's future initiatives and overall performance.As Reckitt Benckiser continues to leverage its expertise in consumer health, this new board membership for Harry Kirsch at Galderma highlights the interconnectedness of the healthcare and consumer sectors, potentially leading to collaborative opportunities that benefit both companies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER AND MCCORMICK FORGE $44.8 BILLION FOOD INDUSTRY POWERHOUSE

In a landmark deal, Unilever Plc has agreed to merge its food business with spice manufacturer McCormick & Co. in a transaction valued at $44.8 billion. This acquisition is set to create a formidable player in the global market for seasonings, sauces, and condiments. The announcement was made on 11th May 2026, highlighting a strategic shift for both companies as they aim to enhance their positions within their respective sectors.Under the terms of the agreement, McCormick will provide $15.7 billion in cash along with shares valued at approximately $29.1 billion for a significant portion of Unilever's food operations. As a result, Unilever and its shareholders will own 65% of the newly formed entity, which will include well-known brands such as French’s mustard and Hellmann’s mayonnaise. This merger is expected to allow Unilever to refocus on its core strengths in beauty, personal care, and home care products, while McCormick aims to bolster its presence in the packaged food industry.Despite the strategic advantages presented by this merger, investor reactions have been cautious. McCormick's stock fell significantly, dropping as much as 10% during US trading following the announcement, and closing at a 21% decline year-to-date. Unilever's shares also reflected market skepticism, closing down 7.3% in London, contributing to an almost 14% decline since the beginning of the year. The financial markets' response indicates a level of uncertainty regarding the potential synergies and future profitability stemming from this ambitious merger.This merger marks the largest transaction in the histories of both Unilever and McCormick, underscoring the transformative potential of the deal in reshaping the competitive landscape of the food sector. As both companies navigate this transition, market analysts will be closely monitoring how the integration of Unilever's food brands into McCormick's portfolio will unfold and impact their respective market positions.The strategic rationale behind the merger is clear: by combining their resources and expertise, Unilever and McCormick aim to create a powerhouse capable of competing more effectively on a global scale. Investors will be keen to see how this merger will play out in the coming months, particularly in terms of operational efficiencies, brand management, and overall market performance.

+ UNILEVER (ULVR) STAKEHOLDERS URGE ESG FOCUS IN MCCORMICK MERGER

Investors in Unilever (LSE:ULVR) are increasingly pushing for guarantees that the company will uphold its environmental and anti-deforestation commitments following the announced merger of its food business with McCormick (NYSE:MKC). The transaction, valued at $65 billion, was disclosed in March and aims to merge Unilever’s food division with that of McCormick, thereby creating a formidable global food enterprise that includes well-known brands such as Hellmann’s mayonnaise and Cholula hot sauce. This merger is expected to significantly amplify McCormick's scale while introducing a more complex global supply chain linked to agriculture and smallholder farming operations. The date of this announcement was 8th May 2026.Shareholders are particularly concerned about how the newly formed entity will manage its sourcing policies and environmental standards. Vemund Olsen, a senior analyst at Norwegian asset manager Storebrand, emphasized the importance of maintaining best practices in deforestation-free sourcing policies. Storebrand is among the top 100 shareholders in Unilever and also holds McCormick shares. Olsen stated, "We will be seeking assurances about the intention of the combined company to uphold and build upon best practice with regard to deforestation-free sourcing of commodities." These standards are critical and include avoiding sourcing from deforested land and ensuring complete traceability of commodities.The disparity in sustainability disclosure regulations between the U.S. and Europe could also complicate matters. Unlike Unilever, McCormick is not bound by the stringent sustainability disclosure requirements that apply in Europe. Companies with significant European operations will eventually need to comply with EU sustainability reporting standards, but this will take time, leading to a transition period where disclosure levels may rely heavily on voluntary corporate commitments. Cailin Dendas, environmental health program senior coordinator at the shareholder advocacy group As You Sow, warned that if Unilever and McCormick do not prioritize sustainability, it could pose considerable risks for shareholders and the new entity.Despite these concerns, Unilever is expected to retain a significant influence in the merged company, holding approximately 10% ownership and four board seats. However, smaller investors may find their ability to influence strategic decisions and governance limited. When asked about its strategy for ensuring McCormick adheres to similar sustainability standards, a Unilever spokesperson indicated that the company is actively collaborating with McCormick to transition its food-related sustainability programs and commitments as the merger progresses.Additionally, McCormick will face pressure to enhance its sustainability capabilities in light of the merger. Hannah Schalk, an analyst at ESG ratings provider Sustainalytics, noted that McCormick currently carries a 'medium-risk' sustainability exposure. The com

+ UNILEVER WEIGHS SEPARATION OF FOOD ASSETS AMID PORTFOLIO SHIFT

Unilever Plc is contemplating a separation of its food assets as part of a broader strategy to enhance growth in its beauty, personal care, and wellbeing brands. This exploration comes as the Anglo-Dutch consumer goods giant evaluates its extensive portfolio. On 29th April 2026, sources familiar with the discussions revealed that Unilever is engaging with advisers to assess various options, including the potential divestment of its food business, which includes well-known brands such as Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes.The deliberations are in their preliminary stages, with Unilever considering multiple pathways, including a full spin-off of its food operations or retaining select flagship brands while divesting the remainder. However, it is unlikely that any formal decision will be made before 2027. Industry insiders estimate that a transaction involving Unilever's food brands could be valued at tens of billions of dollars, although the company has yet to finalize its course of action.Unilever's food division, which includes products like Colman's condiments and the divisive Marmite spread, has seen its shares fluctuate marginally, trading 0.7% lower recently and placing the company's market capitalization at approximately £107 billion ($142 billion). Under the leadership of CEO Fernando Fernandez, Unilever is undergoing a transformative phase, having already spun off its ice cream division last year, retaining a minority stake that it intends to gradually sell.Analysts suggest that Fernandez's inclination towards beauty brands is evident, with Barclays analyst Warren Ackerman noting that while the direction is clear, the timing for a food business separation might not be optimal given the current market conditions. Unilever has previously divested other food assets, including its global spreads division and snack brands, leaving it with around €1 billion to €1.5 billion in local food brands still to be disposed of.The challenges facing major food companies like Unilever are significant, as consumers increasingly seek budget-friendly options and gravitate toward private-label products. In contrast, the beauty sector has emerged as a robust growth avenue, appealing to a diverse consumer base. As Unilever continues to refine its focus on brands such as Dove and Liquid IV, the potential separation of its food assets could reshape its future direction and investment landscape.

urn:contentItem:6JK2-K5D3-S2R3-3166-00000-00SC JOHNSON & SON - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:09ZLexisNexisSTOCKSC JOHNSON & SON - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: SC JOHNSON & SON

PermID4298009420Websitehttp://www.scjohnson.com/en/home.aspx?directLink=yIndustryManufacturer of household products

ACTIVITIES:

SC Johnson & Son, founded in 1886 in Racine, Wisconsin, is a family-owned consumer goods company. With $11 billion in revenue and 13,000 employees, it manufactures household brands like Windex, Glade, and Ziploc, operating in 70 countries. SC Johnson emphasizes sustainability, using renewable energy and reducing plastic waste. Its innovation center develops eco-friendly products, and its transparency in ingredient disclosure sets industry standards. Led by H. Fisk Johnson, the companys philanthropy includes land conservation and education grants. SC Johnsons commitment to quality and environmental responsibility reinforces its global leadership in household goods.

+ SC JOHNSON & SON [3 News That Matters]

+ SC JOHNSON EXPANDS OUTDOOR OFFERINGS WITH NEW OFF! AND RAID PRODUCTS

SC Johnson & Son is helping customers prepare for the outdoor season with the launch of several new products under its OFF! and Raid brands. As consumers gear up for spring and summer activities, the company has unveiled a new line of outdoor products designed to keep patios, lawns, and gardens bug-free. This announcement was made on 7th May 2026.The new offerings from SC Johnson in the OFF! insect repellent family include various formulations aimed at controlling mosquitoes, ants, and wasps. These products are tailored for spring hosting, gardening, and enhancing outdoor spaces, making them a timely addition to the market as warmer weather approaches. Prices for these outdoor products start at $5, appealing to a broad range of consumers looking to enjoy their backyards without the nuisance of insects.In addition to the new OFF! and Raid products, SC Johnson is making strategic moves in its acquisition strategy. The company has announced an agreement to acquire the global Space Bag brand from ITW, which is expected to be finalized in the coming months, pending U.S. regulatory approvals. Financial terms of this acquisition have not been disclosed, but SC Johnson is actively developing transition plans for the brand.Further expanding its portfolio, SC Johnson has also acquired Babyganics, a rapidly growing personal care brand focused on family-oriented products. Founded in 2004, Babyganics offers a wide range of items including skin care, bath care, insect repellent, and cleaning products. This acquisition aligns with SC Johnson's strategy to enhance its presence in the family and personal care market, reinforcing its commitment to providing safe and effective products for consumers.As SC Johnson continues to innovate and expand its product lines, the company is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for outdoor and family care products. The introduction of new OFF! and Raid products, along with strategic acquisitions, underscores SC Johnson's commitment to delivering quality solutions that meet the evolving needs of consumers.

+ SC JOHNSON PARTNERS WITH EARTHECHO INTERNATIONAL TO COMBAT PLASTIC WASTE

SC Johnson & Son is continuing its commitment to environmental sustainability through a partnership with EarthEcho International, aimed at combating plastic waste. The initiative marks the third year of collaboration between the two organizations and was announced on the 31st of March, 2026. In this latest phase, the Marine Plastics Ambassadors program will expand its reach by launching its largest cohort yet, empowering young advocates to tackle the pressing issue of plastic pollution affecting ecosystems worldwide.The Marine Plastics Ambassadors program seeks to provide participants with advocacy training and opportunities for policy engagement. Young changemakers will be equipped with essential tools and mentorship, enabling them to devise scalable solutions tailored to their local communities. This approach aims to actively engage participants in the fight against plastic waste and strengthen environmental protections across the nation. Philippe Cousteau, Jr., founder of EarthEcho International, emphasized the importance of empowering youth to drive change, stating, "The Marine Plastics Ambassadors are leading the way, and EarthEcho is proud to build a community of practice that empowers them to change the world."Fisk Johnson, Chairman and CEO of SC Johnson, expressed optimism about the involvement of young people in addressing plastic pollution. He remarked, "It's encouraging to see young people stepping forward to help tackle this important issue. As a company, we advocate for smart government regulation as a crucial tool in tackling the plastic waste crisis. We're pleased to partner with EarthEcho to support this next generation of leaders in their advocacy efforts."Participants in the Marine Plastics Ambassadors program will gather in Washington, D.C., this summer to advance their policy priorities and engage with legislators. The meeting aims to demonstrate the effectiveness of youth-driven solutions in reducing plastic pollution. With over 430 million tons of plastic produced globally each year, SC Johnson’s support for EarthEcho’s initiatives underscores the urgency of addressing plastic waste, particularly as projections indicate that plastic entering oceans could nearly triple by 2040.As environmental concerns mount, the partnership between SC Johnson and EarthEcho International signifies a proactive step towards mobilizing future generations. The program not only provides essential training but also fosters a network of advocates dedicated to creating impactful change. For more information on the Marine Plastics Ambassadors and their initiatives, visit EarthEcho's official website.

+ SC JOHNSON UNVEILS NEW OFF! DEEP WOODS MAX AEROSOL FOR OUTDOOR ENTHUSIASTS

SC Johnson & Son has launched a new product in its OFF! Deep Woods insect repellent line, introducing the Deep Woods MAX aerosol. The new formulation is designed specifically for outdoor users seeking extended protection against insects. On 2nd April 2026, SC Johnson announced that the Deep Woods MAX aerosol is a picaridin-based formula that effectively repels mosquitoes and ticks for up to 10 hours.The OFF! Deep Woods MAX aerosol is marketed as fragrance-free and non-greasy, making it suitable for those engaged in outdoor activities. Additionally, the product is touted as safe to use around common outdoor equipment, including fishing lines, sporting gear, and painted surfaces, which is a significant consideration for outdoor enthusiasts and anglers.This product launch coincides with SC Johnson's new sponsorship arrangement, wherein the OFF! brand becomes the official mosquito and tick repellent of Major League Fishing (MLF) for the year 2026. The partnership will feature brand presence at various MLF events and include a sponsored angler team participating in the four 2026 MLF Team Series events, enhancing SC Johnson's visibility within the fishing community.The collaboration aims to leverage the popularity of fishing as a recreational activity while promoting the benefits of the OFF! Deep Woods MAX aerosol. SC Johnson's strategic approach to align with MLF is expected to boost brand awareness and potentially drive sales among outdoor enthusiasts who prioritize effective insect protection.As the outdoor activity market continues to grow, SC Johnson's introduction of the OFF! Deep Woods MAX aerosol addresses a specific need for effective insect repellents, reinforcing the company's commitment to providing innovative solutions for consumers. This product launch and sponsorship are poised to enhance SC Johnson's position in the competitive insect repellent market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE [3 News That Matters]

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE NAMES NICOLAS TRAN DINH AS NEW APAC DIGITAL & MEDIA DIRECTOR

Colgate-Palmolive Company has announced the appointment of Nicolas Tran Dinh as the new APAC Digital & Media Director. In this role, Dinh will be responsible for spearheading full-funnel media strategies, geographic operations, and digital transformation initiatives across the Asia-Pacific region. This strategic move is expected to enhance Colgate-Palmolive's digital presence and streamline its marketing efforts in one of the fastest-growing markets.The announcement was made on 13th May 2026, highlighting the company's commitment to adapting its marketing and media strategies to meet the evolving demands of consumers in the digital age. With a focus on leveraging data analytics and innovative digital solutions, Dinh aims to strengthen Colgate-Palmolive's brand engagement and drive growth across diverse markets within the APAC region.Dinh brings a wealth of experience in digital marketing and media management to Colgate-Palmolive, having previously held leadership roles in major multinational companies. His expertise in digital transformation will be crucial as the company navigates the complexities of the modern advertising landscape, especially in the wake of recent shifts in consumer behavior and media consumption patterns.Colgate-Palmolive's decision to elevate Dinh underscores its strategic priority of integrating digital technologies into its core operations. The company is keen on optimizing its marketing spend while ensuring that its digital initiatives resonate effectively with the target audience. As digital advertising continues to evolve, the appointment of a dedicated leader in this space signals the company's proactive approach to maintaining its competitive edge.As Dinh steps into this critical role, industry observers will be watching closely to see how Colgate-Palmolive adapts its strategies in response to the ongoing challenges and opportunities in the digital marketplace. With rising competition and the need for innovative marketing solutions, Dinh's leadership could play a pivotal role in shaping the future of Colgate-Palmolive's brand presence in the APAC region.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE LAUNCHES 'EVERY COLGATE SMILE HAS A STORY' CAMPAIGN

MUMBAI, India – Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited is embarking on a new journey with the launch of its storytelling campaign, 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story.' This initiative celebrates its 90-year legacy in India, where the iconic Colgate tube has become an integral part of countless households, particularly among mothers who view it as a trusted member of the family. This announcement comes on 6th May 2026, marking nearly a century of the brand's presence in the country.The campaign is designed to highlight the profound connection that Indian mothers have with Colgate, portraying the universal desire to protect their families. The series delves into the lives of real people, showcasing their stories and the impact of Colgate's products. The first film, 'Raised by Sweets,' introduces viewers to Vertica Khandelwal's family in Dehradun, who have been creating exquisite sweets for 125 years. As she navigates the joys of sharing sweets with her children, her confidence in Colgate's protective benefits serves as a reassuring backdrop.At the heart of the campaign lies the sentiment of a mother's peace of mind, reinforced by Colgate's scientific advancements. The company emphasizes its Arginine Calcium Boost Technology, which is designed to offer 24-hour cavity protection. Gunjit Jain, Executive Vice President – Marketing at Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited, expressed that this campaign goes beyond traditional advertising, reinforcing the trust that mothers have placed in Colgate over the decades.Colgate-Palmolive aims to amplify the 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' series across various platforms including television, digital media, and social channels. The initiative aligns with the company's broader mission to foster a healthier future for people and the planet, showcasing its commitment to sustainability and community well-being. The brand has made significant strides in reducing plastic waste, promoting recyclability, and enhancing oral health education for children through its Colgate Bright Smiles, Bright Futures® program.As it enters this new chapter, Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited continues to focus on innovation and inclusivity, aspiring to create a lasting impact on both consumers and the environment. This storytelling platform not only aims to celebrate its rich heritage but also to connect with the next generation of families, ensuring that the Colgate legacy continues to thrive.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE CELEBRATES 90 YEARS OF TRUST IN INDIA

Colgate-Palmolive Company has marked a significant milestone in India, celebrating 90 years of being a trusted part of Indian households. This iconic brand, known for its oral care products, is now showcasing its legacy through the 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' campaign. Launched on the 6th of May, 2026, the campaign aims to highlight the emotional connections families have with the brand and its products, reinforcing its position as a leader in oral health solutions.The 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' campaign captures real-life narratives, emphasizing the bond between mothers and their children, driven by the instinct to protect. One such story features Vertica Khandelwal from Dehradun, who juggles cultural heritage and the importance of dental health, demonstrating how Colgate provides reassurance with its slogan 'Colgate Sambhal Lega'. This campaign creatively blends emotional storytelling with the scientific advancements that Colgate-Palmolive has pioneered in oral care.Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited continues to lead the oral care market by combining emotional resonance with scientific integrity. The brand's commitment to innovation is evidenced by its Arginine + Calcium Boost Technology, which delivers 24-hour cavity protection. As the number one oral care brand in India, Colgate is dedicated to offering reliable solutions that meet the needs of its consumers while maintaining a focus on sustainable and inclusive growth.This campaign not only emphasizes the brand's heritage but also reflects Colgate-Palmolive's understanding of the importance of trust in consumer relationships. By connecting with families through relatable stories, the brand reinforces its promise of protection and care, which has been a cornerstone of its identity in the Indian market for nearly a century.As Colgate-Palmolive continues to evolve its marketing strategies, the 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' campaign serves as a reminder of the brand's enduring impact on Indian households. By fostering emotional connections and emphasizing their scientific advancements, Colgate-Palmolive aims to strengthen its legacy while ensuring that it remains a trusted partner in oral health for generations to come.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PROCTER AND GAMBLE [3 News That Matters]

+ PROCTER & GAMBLE SECURES TOP SPOT IN HOUSEHOLD PRODUCTS ON FORTUNE'S 2026 INNOVATION LIST

Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) has garnered the number one position in the Household Products Industry on Fortune's 2026 America's Most Innovative Companies list, a title it has maintained for three consecutive years. The announcement was made on 23rd April 2026, emphasizing the company's strength in Product Innovation among a field of 300 leading innovators in the United States.The Fortune ranking, produced in collaboration with Statista, evaluates companies across three critical dimensions: Product Innovation, Process Innovation, and Innovation Culture. This comprehensive assessment ensures that Procter & Gamble's innovative capabilities are recognized in a rigorous manner, placing the company among America's most elite organizations with a combined revenue exceeding $12.5 trillion.Victor Aguilar, Chief Research, Development & Innovation Officer at Procter & Gamble, stated, "This recognition is a testament to our incredible organization around the world and their commitment to pushing the boundaries of what's possible, transforming how we innovate." He emphasized the company's consumer-first approach, focusing on deeply understanding consumer needs and employing advanced science and technology to develop solutions that resonate with customers.Procter & Gamble's sustained recognition in Product Innovation reflects substantial investments across its diverse brand portfolio. The company continues to integrate innovation strategies across various categories, including Fabric Care, Home Care, and Personal Health Care. This approach enables Procter & Gamble to leverage its extensive intellectual property portfolio, translating scientific advancements into products that enhance consumer lives.As an industry leader, Procter & Gamble's commitment to innovation is further demonstrated in its robust performance in the Fortune 500 rankings. The company's diverse range of trusted brands, including Tide®, Gillette®, and Olay®, solidifies its position as a key player in the global market, serving consumers in approximately 70 countries. For more information on Procter & Gamble and its initiatives, please visit https://www.pg.com.

+ P&G PRO REVIVES MR. CLEAN: A NEW ERA FOR PROFESSIONAL CLEANING SOLUTIONS

CINCINNATI--Mr. Clean is back from retirement—and with Mr. Clean PRO, he’s ready for hire as businesses’ most dependable new employee with a full cleaning portfolio to get the job done. Today, 10th April 2026, Mr. Clean PRO is introducing the next generation of professional cleaning to help businesses clean more efficiently and effectively, making him the employee businesses can always count on.Cleaning is a critical aspect of operations across various sectors, including restaurants, retail, and offices. Mr. Clean PRO aims to address frequent challenges faced by professionals, particularly regarding the effectiveness of current surface cleaning solutions. With commercial-grade formulas specifically designed to tackle grease, stains, and grime, Mr. Clean PRO offers businesses a reliable option to enhance their cleaning operations.Bryan Smith, Vice President North America at Procter & Gamble PRO, emphasized the importance of providing solutions that meet the needs of professionals. He stated, 'With Mr. Clean PRO, we’re helping to address those areas of need because choosing the right surface and floor cleaner can make a big difference.' This sentiment reflects a growing demand for effective cleaning solutions that allow businesses to maintain high standards of cleanliness and efficiency.The new Mr. Clean PRO line includes several innovative products tailored for commercial use, such as the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial All Surface Cleaner and the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial Deep Cleaner with Bleach. These products are specifically engineered to deliver the same dependability and results that the Mr. Clean brand is known for in household cleaning, but with enhanced performance for commercial environments.Mr. Clean PRO products are available nationwide through various distributors and retailers, both in stores and online. With sizes ranging from 1-gallon to 32oz, businesses can easily access the cleaning solutions they need to keep operations running smoothly. For more information, visit pgpro.com and follow P&G PRO on social media for updates on future innovations and offerings.

+ P&G EXPANDS BRAND ENGAGEMENT THROUGH WNBA PARTNERSHIP

Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) has announced a multi-year, multi-brand partnership with the Women’s National Basketball Association (WNBA) that aims to enhance brand relevance and engage a broader audience. This strategic collaboration is set to include various flagship P&G brands in athlete collaborations, league events, and digital storytelling campaigns, marking a significant step for the company within the realm of women’s sports.The partnership, revealed on the 9th of April, 2026, is designed to deepen engagement with professional women's basketball and its rapidly growing fan base. As P&G increasingly focuses on brand relevance and emotional connections rather than just product placement on shelves, this initiative aligns with current trends in sports marketing and digital media. By tapping into the rising visibility of women's sports, P&G aims to connect with younger and more diverse consumers, positioning its legacy brands favorably in a competitive marketplace.Looking ahead, the effectiveness of these WNBA campaigns will be closely monitored by investors, particularly in terms of their impact on brand equity and customer loyalty. Procter & Gamble's marketing spend in sports and entertainment will likely be influenced by the execution and scale of these initiatives. Investors are keen to see how this partnership will contribute to the company’s broader strategy to connect with underrepresented demographics, especially as the WNBA continues to gain popularity.Despite the optimistic outlook, there are considerations for investors regarding P&G's financial health and its commitment to disciplined balance sheet management. With the company’s share price currently trading at approximately 13% below analyst expectations, concerns about short-term momentum, including a recent 6.6% decline over the past month, may raise questions about the timing of this marketing investment. Investors will be looking for clear disclosures from Procter & Gamble regarding the returns from this partnership in upcoming earnings reports.In summary, the collaboration between Procter & Gamble and the WNBA represents a strategic move to bolster brand storytelling and visibility among a younger audience. As this partnership unfolds, stakeholders will be keen on how effectively it reinforces P&G's established brands and appeals to a fast-growing market segment while managing the associated financial risks.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RB [3 News That Matters]

+ RECKITT BENCKISER'S DIGITAL SHIFT IN CHINA GAINS INVESTOR ATTENTION

Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC is navigating a pivotal moment as it adapts to China's fast-evolving e-commerce landscape. Kris Licht, CEO of Reckitt, emphasizes the significance of this transition, describing it as "the most profound channel shift" he has encountered. As of 9th May 2026, Reckitt has reported that e-commerce and social commerce have surged to account for roughly 80% of its sales in China, a dramatic increase from 30% six years ago. The company’s revenue from the region is nearing £1 billion, buoyed by 11 consecutive quarters of double-digit growth, as Licht revealed in an interview with Semafor.The timing of Reckitt's strategic pivot comes as the company aims to reassure investors about its digital sales initiatives, accelerated product cycles, and the integration of artificial intelligence. This comes in the wake of a challenging year marked by soft demand for cold-and-flu products, rising costs driven by oil prices, and ongoing struggles in Russia. Reckitt's upcoming "Focus On: Digital Science" virtual investor event on 14th May is expected to showcase how its digital and R&D teams are leveraging technology to foster innovation and drive category growth, providing a timely backdrop for Licht to reshape the narrative around Reckitt's operations in China.Despite these strategic moves, Reckitt's stock performance has faced scrutiny. The company recently disclosed a buyback of 190,500 ordinary shares, totaling about £9 million, as part of its efforts to maintain capital returns. However, there are lingering concerns about Reckitt's financial outlook, particularly after missing first-quarter revenue expectations and forecasting a dip in first-half margins. Analysts like Harsharan Mann from Aviva Investors have characterized the quarter as one of "broad-based muted growth," while JPMorgan's Celine Pannuti has highlighted uncertainty regarding Reckitt's ability to meet full-year targets, particularly in emerging markets.The competitive landscape is further complicated by rising costs affecting the entire consumer staples sector. Companies like Unilever have announced plans to implement modest price increases to counteract inflation, while Reckitt has also signaled potential margin pressures. The shift in consumer preferences towards lower-priced private-label goods raises additional challenges for Reckitt, especially as some shoppers are already exploring alternatives to its branded health and hygiene products. Market analysts, including Zavier Wong from eToro, suggest that companies that have hedged against these pressures are better positioned to weather the storm.Moreover, Reckitt’s operations in Russia remain a point of contention, as new EU sanctions have complicated its product offerings. The company's local arm is actively working on new product development and filing for new intellectual property to fill gaps left by the sanctions. While Licht remains optimistic that accelerated moves could mitigate some of these challenge

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP POSITIONED FOR GROWTH AS AIR FRESHENERS MARKET EXPANDS

The air fresheners market is poised for significant expansion, with projections indicating a rise to $19.75 billion by 2030. This growth is underpinned by urbanization and innovation in fragrance technologies. As of 27th April 2026, the market is expected to grow from $14.36 billion in 2025 to $15.43 billion in 2026, reflecting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.5%. The increasing awareness of indoor ambiance, heightened household spending, and greater retail accessibility are key contributors to this trend.The anticipated growth trajectory of the air fresheners market is bolstered by factors such as a rising preference for enhancing home comfort and a growing demand for products that improve lifestyle quality. Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC, alongside competitors like Godrej Consumer Products and Procter & Gamble, is actively engaging in the development of innovative fragrance solutions. The market's expansion is further fueled by a focus on wellness-driven environments and the enhancement of indoor air quality, as consumers become more health-conscious.Urbanization plays a pivotal role in the increasing demand for air fresheners, as more individuals migrate to urban centers in search of improved living standards. This shift in lifestyle drives the need for indoor fragrance solutions, particularly in densely populated areas. Furthermore, rising air pollution levels, primarily from industrialization and vehicle emissions, have created a heightened demand for air fresheners that can mask unpleasant odors and improve perceived indoor air quality.In response to growing environmental concerns, Reckitt Benckiser Group and other major players in the air fresheners market are innovating with advanced aerosol dispersion technologies. These innovations focus on precise and efficient fragrance delivery while minimizing environmental impacts. For example, Everspray's micro-diffusion aerosol system exemplifies this trend by delivering ultra-fine microdroplets for consistent scent coverage with minimal waste.Regionally, Europe led the air fresheners market in 2025, while the Asia-Pacific region is anticipated to emerge as the fastest-growing market in the coming years. As companies like Reckitt Benckiser Group continue to develop sustainable solutions and adapt to evolving consumer preferences, they are well-positioned to capitalize on the anticipated market growth. Investors are keenly observing these developments as they evaluate future opportunities in this expanding sector.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER'S HARRY KIRSCH JOINS GALDERMA BOARD

LONDON - Reckitt Benckiser Group plc announced that Harry Kirsch, one of its non-executive directors, has been appointed to the board of Galderma (SIX:GALD) as of 28th April 2026. This strategic move is seen as a significant step for both companies, given Galderma's focus on dermatological products and Reckitt's extensive portfolio in consumer health and hygiene.The appointment was disclosed in accordance with Listing Rule 9.6.14 of the Financial Conduct Authority’s Listing Rules, as detailed in a press release from Reckitt Benckiser. While the company has not provided additional information regarding Kirsch’s specific role at Galderma or the terms of his appointment, his experience is expected to bring valuable insights to the Swiss-based firm.Kirsch continues to serve as a non-executive director at Reckitt Benckiser, a leading consumer health and hygiene products company known for its prominent brands including Dettol, Lysol, Nurofen, and Mucinex. His dual roles may foster synergies between Reckitt Benckiser and Galderma, particularly in areas of product innovation and market expansion.Galderma, which operates in the highly competitive skincare and dermatology sector, has been positioning itself for growth, and the addition of Kirsch could signal a commitment to enhancing its governance and strategic direction. Investors will be keen to see how this appointment influences Galderma's future initiatives and overall performance.As Reckitt Benckiser continues to leverage its expertise in consumer health, this new board membership for Harry Kirsch at Galderma highlights the interconnectedness of the healthcare and consumer sectors, potentially leading to collaborative opportunities that benefit both companies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER AND MCCORMICK FORGE $44.8 BILLION FOOD INDUSTRY POWERHOUSE

In a landmark deal, Unilever Plc has agreed to merge its food business with spice manufacturer McCormick & Co. in a transaction valued at $44.8 billion. This acquisition is set to create a formidable player in the global market for seasonings, sauces, and condiments. The announcement was made on 11th May 2026, highlighting a strategic shift for both companies as they aim to enhance their positions within their respective sectors.Under the terms of the agreement, McCormick will provide $15.7 billion in cash along with shares valued at approximately $29.1 billion for a significant portion of Unilever's food operations. As a result, Unilever and its shareholders will own 65% of the newly formed entity, which will include well-known brands such as French’s mustard and Hellmann’s mayonnaise. This merger is expected to allow Unilever to refocus on its core strengths in beauty, personal care, and home care products, while McCormick aims to bolster its presence in the packaged food industry.Despite the strategic advantages presented by this merger, investor reactions have been cautious. McCormick's stock fell significantly, dropping as much as 10% during US trading following the announcement, and closing at a 21% decline year-to-date. Unilever's shares also reflected market skepticism, closing down 7.3% in London, contributing to an almost 14% decline since the beginning of the year. The financial markets' response indicates a level of uncertainty regarding the potential synergies and future profitability stemming from this ambitious merger.This merger marks the largest transaction in the histories of both Unilever and McCormick, underscoring the transformative potential of the deal in reshaping the competitive landscape of the food sector. As both companies navigate this transition, market analysts will be closely monitoring how the integration of Unilever's food brands into McCormick's portfolio will unfold and impact their respective market positions.The strategic rationale behind the merger is clear: by combining their resources and expertise, Unilever and McCormick aim to create a powerhouse capable of competing more effectively on a global scale. Investors will be keen to see how this merger will play out in the coming months, particularly in terms of operational efficiencies, brand management, and overall market performance.

+ UNILEVER (ULVR) STAKEHOLDERS URGE ESG FOCUS IN MCCORMICK MERGER

Investors in Unilever (LSE:ULVR) are increasingly pushing for guarantees that the company will uphold its environmental and anti-deforestation commitments following the announced merger of its food business with McCormick (NYSE:MKC). The transaction, valued at $65 billion, was disclosed in March and aims to merge Unilever’s food division with that of McCormick, thereby creating a formidable global food enterprise that includes well-known brands such as Hellmann’s mayonnaise and Cholula hot sauce. This merger is expected to significantly amplify McCormick's scale while introducing a more complex global supply chain linked to agriculture and smallholder farming operations. The date of this announcement was 8th May 2026.Shareholders are particularly concerned about how the newly formed entity will manage its sourcing policies and environmental standards. Vemund Olsen, a senior analyst at Norwegian asset manager Storebrand, emphasized the importance of maintaining best practices in deforestation-free sourcing policies. Storebrand is among the top 100 shareholders in Unilever and also holds McCormick shares. Olsen stated, "We will be seeking assurances about the intention of the combined company to uphold and build upon best practice with regard to deforestation-free sourcing of commodities." These standards are critical and include avoiding sourcing from deforested land and ensuring complete traceability of commodities.The disparity in sustainability disclosure regulations between the U.S. and Europe could also complicate matters. Unlike Unilever, McCormick is not bound by the stringent sustainability disclosure requirements that apply in Europe. Companies with significant European operations will eventually need to comply with EU sustainability reporting standards, but this will take time, leading to a transition period where disclosure levels may rely heavily on voluntary corporate commitments. Cailin Dendas, environmental health program senior coordinator at the shareholder advocacy group As You Sow, warned that if Unilever and McCormick do not prioritize sustainability, it could pose considerable risks for shareholders and the new entity.Despite these concerns, Unilever is expected to retain a significant influence in the merged company, holding approximately 10% ownership and four board seats. However, smaller investors may find their ability to influence strategic decisions and governance limited. When asked about its strategy for ensuring McCormick adheres to similar sustainability standards, a Unilever spokesperson indicated that the company is actively collaborating with McCormick to transition its food-related sustainability programs and commitments as the merger progresses.Additionally, McCormick will face pressure to enhance its sustainability capabilities in light of the merger. Hannah Schalk, an analyst at ESG ratings provider Sustainalytics, noted that McCormick currently carries a 'medium-risk' sustainability exposure. The com

+ UNILEVER WEIGHS SEPARATION OF FOOD ASSETS AMID PORTFOLIO SHIFT

Unilever Plc is contemplating a separation of its food assets as part of a broader strategy to enhance growth in its beauty, personal care, and wellbeing brands. This exploration comes as the Anglo-Dutch consumer goods giant evaluates its extensive portfolio. On 29th April 2026, sources familiar with the discussions revealed that Unilever is engaging with advisers to assess various options, including the potential divestment of its food business, which includes well-known brands such as Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes.The deliberations are in their preliminary stages, with Unilever considering multiple pathways, including a full spin-off of its food operations or retaining select flagship brands while divesting the remainder. However, it is unlikely that any formal decision will be made before 2027. Industry insiders estimate that a transaction involving Unilever's food brands could be valued at tens of billions of dollars, although the company has yet to finalize its course of action.Unilever's food division, which includes products like Colman's condiments and the divisive Marmite spread, has seen its shares fluctuate marginally, trading 0.7% lower recently and placing the company's market capitalization at approximately £107 billion ($142 billion). Under the leadership of CEO Fernando Fernandez, Unilever is undergoing a transformative phase, having already spun off its ice cream division last year, retaining a minority stake that it intends to gradually sell.Analysts suggest that Fernandez's inclination towards beauty brands is evident, with Barclays analyst Warren Ackerman noting that while the direction is clear, the timing for a food business separation might not be optimal given the current market conditions. Unilever has previously divested other food assets, including its global spreads division and snack brands, leaving it with around €1 billion to €1.5 billion in local food brands still to be disposed of.The challenges facing major food companies like Unilever are significant, as consumers increasingly seek budget-friendly options and gravitate toward private-label products. In contrast, the beauty sector has emerged as a robust growth avenue, appealing to a diverse consumer base. As Unilever continues to refine its focus on brands such as Dove and Liquid IV, the potential separation of its food assets could reshape its future direction and investment landscape.

NASDAQ END-OF-DAY STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

MADISON, Thursday, May 14 2026 - Alliant Energy Corporation's (NASDAQ: LNT $72.47) stock price increased 17.0c (0.2%) from its previous trading session to close at $72.47.

Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 213.4 points (0.7%) in the day, the relative price change was -0.5%.

There were 1,178,050 shares worth $85.4 million traded today; the volume was 0.6 times the average daily trading volume of 2.1 million shares.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.
