value.Title: DTE Energy Company 's 10-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Alliant Energy Corporation

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-DPW3-RXW9-W100-00000-00DTE Energy Company's 10-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Alliant Energy Corporation2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:08ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company's 10-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Alliant Energy Corporation

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 7.4% annualized over 10 years.

In comparison, Alliant Energy Corporation's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 6.5% annualized over 10 years.

DTE Energy Company's last trading price was $142.44 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 22.1. With an EPS growth rate of 8.4 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 2.63 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Alliant Energy Corporation's last trading price was $72.31 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 23. With an EPS growth rate of 4.9 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 4.7 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

Alliant Energy Corporation is a regulated utility holding company. It provides electric and natural gas services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in the Midwest. The company operates through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its primary revenue stream is electric utility operations, which constituted about 93% of third-quarter 2025 revenues. This segment is also its fastest-growing, with revenues up 13% year-over-year, driven by rate increases, higher sales volumes, and expanding data center demand. Gas utility revenues represent approximately 4% of the total, with other utility and non-utility segments comprising the rest. The gas segment also showed growth, increasing 4% amid seasonal fluctuations.

value.Title: SC JOHNSON & SON - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHN-7S43-SK5D-33CC-00000-00SC JOHNSON & SON - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:09ZLexisNexisSTOCKSC JOHNSON & SON - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: SC JOHNSON & SON

PermID4298009420Websitehttp://www.scjohnson.com/en/home.aspx?directLink=yIndustryManufacturer of household products

ACTIVITIES:

SC Johnson & Son, founded in 1886 in Racine, Wisconsin, is a family-owned consumer goods company. With $11 billion in revenue and 13,000 employees, it manufactures household brands like Windex, Glade, and Ziploc, operating in 70 countries. SC Johnson emphasizes sustainability, using renewable energy and reducing plastic waste. Its innovation center develops eco-friendly products, and its transparency in ingredient disclosure sets industry standards. Led by H. Fisk Johnson, the companys philanthropy includes land conservation and education grants. SC Johnsons commitment to quality and environmental responsibility reinforces its global leadership in household goods.

+ SC JOHNSON & SON [2 News That Matters]

+ YOUTH LEADERS TAKE CENTER STAGE AS SC JOHNSON PARTNERS WITH EARTHECHO TO COMBAT PLASTIC WASTE

SC Johnson & Son and EarthEcho International announced on March 31, 2026 that the Marine Plastics Ambassadors program will expand into its largest cohort yet, the latest phase in their three-year partnership to curb plastic waste.The program will provide participants with advocacy training and opportunities for policy engagement, along with mentorship to help them design scalable solutions for their local communities."The Marine Plastics Ambassadors are leading the way, and EarthEcho is proud to build a community of practice that empowers them to change the world," said Philippe Cousteau, Jr., founder of EarthEcho International."It's encouraging to see young people stepping forward to help tackle this important issue. As a company, we advocate for smart government regulation as a crucial tool in tackling the plastic waste crisis. We're pleased to partner with EarthEcho to support this next generation of leaders in their advocacy efforts," said Fisk Johnson, Chairman and CEO of SC Johnson.Participants will convene in Washington, D.C., this summer to advance policy priorities and engage with legislators, demonstrating the effectiveness of youth-driven solutions. With more than 430 million tons of plastic produced globally each year, and projections that plastic entering oceans could nearly triple by 2040, the partnership underscores the urgency of addressing plastic waste. For more information on the Marine Plastics Ambassadors and their initiatives, visit EarthEcho's official website.

+ OFF! DEEP WOODS MAX AEROSOL DEBUTS AS A 10-HOUR PICARIDIN REPELLENT

SC Johnson and Son introduced Deep Woods MAX aerosol, the latest in OFF! Deep Woods, designed for extended protection. On 2nd April 2026, the company said the picaridin-based formula repels mosquitoes and ticks for up to 10 hours.The product is fragrance-free and non-greasy, and safe to use around common outdoor equipment, including fishing lines, sporting gear, and painted surfaces.The launch aligns with a sponsorship making OFF! the official mosquito and tick repellent of Major League Fishing for 2026. The deal includes a brand presence at several MLF events and a sponsored angler team competing in the four 2026 MLF Team Series events.SC Johnson aims to leverage fishing's popularity to promote OFF! Deep Woods MAX and expects increased brand awareness and sales among outdoor enthusiasts who prioritize effective protection.As the outdoor activity market grows, the new repellent addresses a market need and reinforces SC Johnson's commitment to innovative solutions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE [3 News That Matters]

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) INTRODUCES EVERY COLGATE SMILE HAS A STORY CAMPAIGN BASED ON CONSUMER NARRATIVES

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) Introduces the Real Stories Platform Through Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited to Highlight Family Health in India, with the debut on 4th May 2026. The initiative weaves real consumer narratives into brand messaging and marks a shift from traditional advertising toward emotionally engaging storytelling rooted in Colgate's nearly 90-year presence in Indian homes. The campaign centers on Real People, Real Stories, and Real Impact to resonate with families across TV, digital, and social channels. The first film, 'Raised by Sweets,' features Vertica Khandelwal from Dehradun as she navigates parenting within a 125-year tradition of sweet-making, underscoring dental health concerns and reinforcing the message that Colgate Sambhal Lega will handle it. The program combines emotional storytelling with science, anchored by Strong Teeth featuring Arginine + Calcium Boost Technology to deliver 24-hour cavity protection. Gunjit Jain, Executive Vice President – Marketing at Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited, says the initiative extends beyond traditional advertising to honor the trust mothers have placed in the brand for generations. The series will be amplified across television, digital, and social platforms, highlighting Colgate as a Suraksha Chakra, a protective shield in family life, and aiming to deepen loyalty while attracting new audiences. This approach reflects a broader trend in the consumer goods sector toward storytelling to engage and earn trust.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) SETS UP TOTAL PERIODONTAL HEALTH LAB IN HONG KONG WITH HK DENTAL ASSOCIATION

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) Sets Up TOTAL Periodontal Health Lab in Hong Kong With the Hong Kong Dental Association to promote gum health and preventive care at a community scale. The eight-day event runs from 1st May 2026 to 7th May 2026 at Metro City Plaza in Tseung Kwan O and includes Hong Kong's first community-wide oral health assessment, supported by PARKnSHOP and Watsons under the AS Watson Group. Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) will work with NGOs to offer interactive activities and professional consultations, with dentists and pharmacists providing assessments. The campaign emphasizes daily oral hygiene as a preventive measure against gum disease. Through the Bright Smiles, Bright Futures program, a Smile Power Station will collect smiles for charity, enabling toothpaste donations to the Hong Kong Dental Association's outreach programs for each smile recorded

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) POSTS PROGRESS TOWARD 2030 SUSTAINABILITY & SOCIAL IMPACT STRATEGY

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) Posts Progress Toward 2030 Sustainability & Social Impact Strategy, unveiled Apr. 28, 2026, as a five-year roadmap designed to strengthen resilience and create value for people, pets, and the planet. The plan builds on the 2025 strategy, highlighting milestones such as the 2019 introduction of the first recyclable toothpaste tube, and current packaging progress of 97% of toothpaste SKUs in North America and 98% globally in recyclable tubes, with a target of 100% by 2030. The Bright Smiles, Bright Futures program has reached nearly two billion children and families across more than 100 countries since 1991.The 2030 Strategy centers on three pillars: enhancing social impact, improving hygiene habits, and preserving the environment. Colgate-Palmolive aims to establish a climate-resilient value chain, protect forests and biodiversity, and eliminate plastic waste from its product lineup, while ensuring water security in communities and promoting lasting hygiene habits through various initiatives. It also covers pet care as part of a broader commitment to healthier futures for pets and owners.Ann Tracy, Chief Sustainability Officer, notes the strategy is value-driven and science-based, with new targets to be announced later in 2026. She adds that she posted on LinkedIn that the company seeks to drive value creation that fosters long-term growth and resilience for people, pets, and the planet under its "win-win-win" principle.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PROCTER AND GAMBLE [3 News That Matters]

+ THE PROCTER & GAMBLE COMPANY (NYSE:PG) SETS BENCHMARK IN HOUSEHOLD PRODUCTS AS FORTUNE NAMES 2026 AMERICA'S MOST INNOVATIVE COMPANIES

The Procter & Gamble Company (NYSE:PG) Posts the top spot in the Household Products Industry on Fortune's 2026 America's Most Innovative Companies list, a distinction it has held for three consecutive years. The announcement was made on 23rd April 2026, and the Fortune ranking, produced in collaboration with Statista, assesses 300 leading U.S. innovators across Product Innovation, Process Innovation and Innovation Culture, with a combined revenue exceeding $12.5 trillion. The ranking reinforces Procter & Gamble's ongoing investments in product development and process improvements that create value for consumers, spanning Fabric Care, Home Care and Personal Health Care. Victor Aguilar, Chief Research, Development & Innovation Officer, said the recognition highlights the company's global reach and its commitment to pushing the boundaries of what's possible, transforming how we innovate. The company continues to leverage a robust intellectual property portfolio to translate scientific advances into everyday products. Tide, Gillette and Olay anchor a diverse brand lineup that serves consumers in approximately 70 countries. For more information, visit https://www.pg.com

+ MR. CLEAN PRO DEBUTS AS P&G PRO’S COMMERCIAL CLEANING SOLUTION

CINCINNATI—Mr. Clean is back in the professional cleaning arena with a new lineup, Mr. Clean PRO, introduced by Procter & Gamble PRO. The program positions the brand as the dependable, next-generation option for business cleaning needs. Today, 10th April 2026, P&G PRO unveiled the commercial-focused range and its plan to help businesses clean more efficiently.Cleaning is a critical operation across restaurants, retail and offices, and Mr. Clean PRO aims to address the persistent gaps in current surface cleaning solutions with commercial-grade formulas designed for grease, stains and grime.Bryan Smith, Vice President North America at P&G PRO, said, 'With Mr. Clean PRO, we’re helping to address those areas of need because choosing the right surface and floor cleaner can make a big difference.' He noted growing demand for solutions that let businesses maintain high cleanliness and efficiency.The line includes products like the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial All Surface Cleaner and the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial Deep Cleaner with Bleach. These products are engineered to deliver the same dependability relied on in households, but with enhanced performance for commercial environments.Mr. Clean PRO products are available nationwide through distributors and retailers, in sizes from 1 gallon to 32 oz. For more information, visit pgpro.com and follow P&G PRO on social media for updates on future innovations and offerings.

+ P&G BROADENS BRAND ENGAGEMENT WITH WNBA ALLIANCE

Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) announced a multi-year, multi-brand partnership with the WNBA designed to deepen brand relevance and broaden reach, integrating flagship brands into athlete collaborations, league events, and digital storytelling.The partnership was revealed on the 9th of April, 2026, and aims to deepen engagement with professional women’s basketball and its rapidly growing fan base by focusing on emotional connections rather than solely on shelf placement, aligning with trends in sports marketing and digital media.P&G intends to connect with younger and more diverse consumers, positioning its legacy brands favorably as the WNBA gains visibility.Investors will assess the campaigns’ effectiveness in terms of brand equity and customer loyalty, with marketing spend in sports and entertainment likely influenced by execution and scale.However, some concerns about P&G’s financial health persist, as shares trade about 13% below analyst expectations and recently fell 6.6% in the past month, underscoring the need for clear disclosures on the returns from the partnership in forthcoming earnings.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RB [3 News That Matters]

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP PLC (L:RKT) SETS COURSE FOR AIR FRESHENERS GROWTH AS MARKET FORECAST REACHES $19.75 BILLION BY 2030

Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC (L:RKT) and peers in the air fresheners sector pursue a market forecast calling for growth to $19.75 billion by 2030. Global demand remains supported by urbanization and ongoing fragrance technology innovation. As of 27th April 2026, the market is set to rise from $14.36 billion in 2025 to $15.43 billion in 2026, reflecting a 7.5% compound annual growth rate.RB and peers such as Godrej Consumer Products and Procter & Gamble actively pursue new fragrance solutions to capture expanding interest in home ambiance and wellness. The push to improve indoor air quality and lifestyle quality drives demand for air fresheners, aided by higher household spending and broader retail access.Industry dynamics show Europe leading in 2025, with Asia-Pacific projected to be the fastest growing region in the coming years. Companies reinforce growth through sustainable delivery technologies, including Everspray micro-diffusion aerosol systems that deliver ultra-fine droplets with reduced waste, while other innovations minimize environmental impact.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP PLC (L:RKT) HARRY KIRSCH JOINS GALDERMA BOARD, STRENGTHENING GOVERNANCE LINK

Reckitt Benckiser Group plc (L:RKT) has appointed Harry Kirsch, a non-executive director, to the board of Galderma (SIX:GALD) with effect from 28th April 2026. Galderma, a Swiss-based company focused on dermatology products, will gain Kirsch's governance experience.The appointment was disclosed under the Financial Conduct Authority's Listing Rule 9.6.14 in a Reckitt Benckiser press release. Kirsch will continue to serve as a non-executive director at Reckitt, and terms of the Galderma appointment were not disclosed.The move could foster synergies in product development and market expansion, linking Reckitt's consumer health brands with Galderma's dermatology portfolio. Investors will monitor Galderma's governance and strategic direction as Kirsch contributes to its board.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP PLC (L:RKT) RECALIBRATES RUSSIA STRATEGY WITH LOCAL SOURCING AND IP REGISTRATIONS

Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC (L:RKT) is pivoting its Russia strategy by developing new products and registering intellectual property to replace the majority of its existing hygiene portfolio, as of 2026-04-23 11:53:50.836820.The shift follows a soft start to the first quarter of 2026, with near-term headwinds from fluctuating consumer demand and intensified competition in the hygiene sector.The plan emphasizes local manufacturing and sourcing to strengthen resilience and support Reckitt's long-term sustainability goals.Registration of new IP will protect innovations and reinforce its market position in a region where consumer preferences have shifted.Reckitt Benckiser is reviewing its Russia operating framework to navigate market complexities while continuing to deliver value to stakeholders.Analysts say the recalibration could lift financial performance in coming quarters if execution succeeds, with investors watching closely.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER PLC (LSE:ULVR) SIGNALS POTENTIAL SPIN-OFF OF FOOD ARM AS FOCUS SHIFTS TO BEAUTY AND WELLNESS

Unilever PLC (LSE:ULVR) is weighing the separation of its food assets as part of a broader shift toward growing its beauty, personal care and wellbeing brands. On 29th April 2026, advisers are being engaged to assess options, including a potential divestment of the food business that includes Hellmann's and Knorr. The discussions are in early stages, with scenarios ranging from a full spin-off to retaining flagship brands while divesting others, and a formal decision is not expected before 2027. Industry experts estimate a tens-of-billions-of-dollars transaction if a sale proceeds, though no final course has been chosen. The food division, which also covers Colman's and Marmite, has seen shares drift about 0.7% lower, keeping Unilever's market cap around £107 billion ($142 billion). Under Fernandez, the company is sharpening its focus on beauty brands like Dove and products such as Liquid IV, having spun off the ice cream unit last year while retaining a minority stake to be sold gradually. Barclays' Warren Ackerman says the strategic direction is evident, though market conditions may affect the timing of any food separation. Unilever has previously disposed of its global spreads division and several snack brands, leaving roughly €1 billion to €1.5 billion of local food brands still to exit. The move could recalibrate the company's investment trajectory amid a broader shift toward value and private-label offerings in the food sector

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) AI-POWERED PLATFORM SPEEDS PRODUCT CREATION IN BEAUTY AND WELLBEING

Unilever PLC (L:ULVR) On 28th April 2026, the company said it is leveraging artificial intelligence to decode consumer trends and analyze decades of research data, enabling products to be created in days rather than months. The €12.8 billion Beauty & Wellbeing division is deploying AI and machine learning to assess consumer insights 60% faster than traditional methods, with real-time intelligence drawn from more than 1,000 external data sources monthly to inform development decisions.To operationalize these insights, the firm introduced unique AI tools that synthesize findings with its extensive R&D database, reducing formulation cycles from five or six rounds to one or two and shrinking concept development from months to days. The claims generation process has been accelerated by 75%, further improving development throughput.Beyond formulation, Unilever uses AI to mine biological data within its R&D datasets and run complex research at unprecedented speeds. The rollout of virtual cohorts lets scientists simulate responses across demographics, cutting early-stage testing time and costs while preserving essential real-world validation.AI-driven advances have catalyzed product launches such as Hydra Miracle, a range featuring a novel ingredient that improves skin hydration. Meanwhile, Damage Therapy is delivering double-digit growth, illustrating AI's role as a catalyst for creativity and scientific progress, not just efficiency.Chief R&D Officer comments that structured data paired with human creativity is redefining possibilities in research and development, signaling a new era for Unilever's Beauty & Wellbeing pipeline.

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) EXPLORES €29 BILLION FOOD ARM SALE TO MCCORMICK

Unilever PLC (L:ULVR) is evaluating an offer from McCormick & Co. for its food division, valued at about €29 billion ($33 billion). The disclosure was made on Apr. 26, 2026, with Unilever confirming discussions are ongoing and that binding terms remain under negotiation. The proposed transaction would be the largest acquisition in McCormick's history, given its current market capitalization of $14.5 billion, while Unilever stands at £101 billion ($135 billion)

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHF-8HY3-S51B-P1BF-00000-00SC JOHNSON & SON - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:09ZLexisNexisSTOCKSC JOHNSON & SON - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: SC JOHNSON & SON

PermID4298009420Websitehttp://www.scjohnson.com/en/home.aspx?directLink=yIndustryManufacturer of household products

ACTIVITIES:

SC Johnson & Son, founded in 1886 in Racine, Wisconsin, is a family-owned consumer goods company. With $11 billion in revenue and 13,000 employees, it manufactures household brands like Windex, Glade, and Ziploc, operating in 70 countries. SC Johnson emphasizes sustainability, using renewable energy and reducing plastic waste. Its innovation center develops eco-friendly products, and its transparency in ingredient disclosure sets industry standards. Led by H. Fisk Johnson, the companys philanthropy includes land conservation and education grants. SC Johnsons commitment to quality and environmental responsibility reinforces its global leadership in household goods.

+ SC JOHNSON & SON [2 News That Matters]

+ YOUTH LEADERS TAKE CENTER STAGE AS SC JOHNSON PARTNERS WITH EARTHECHO TO COMBAT PLASTIC WASTE

SC Johnson & Son and EarthEcho International announced on March 31, 2026 that the Marine Plastics Ambassadors program will expand into its largest cohort yet, the latest phase in their three-year partnership to curb plastic waste.The program will provide participants with advocacy training and opportunities for policy engagement, along with mentorship to help them design scalable solutions for their local communities."The Marine Plastics Ambassadors are leading the way, and EarthEcho is proud to build a community of practice that empowers them to change the world," said Philippe Cousteau, Jr., founder of EarthEcho International."It's encouraging to see young people stepping forward to help tackle this important issue. As a company, we advocate for smart government regulation as a crucial tool in tackling the plastic waste crisis. We're pleased to partner with EarthEcho to support this next generation of leaders in their advocacy efforts," said Fisk Johnson, Chairman and CEO of SC Johnson.Participants will convene in Washington, D.C., this summer to advance policy priorities and engage with legislators, demonstrating the effectiveness of youth-driven solutions. With more than 430 million tons of plastic produced globally each year, and projections that plastic entering oceans could nearly triple by 2040, the partnership underscores the urgency of addressing plastic waste. For more information on the Marine Plastics Ambassadors and their initiatives, visit EarthEcho's official website.

+ OFF! DEEP WOODS MAX AEROSOL DEBUTS AS A 10-HOUR PICARIDIN REPELLENT

SC Johnson and Son introduced Deep Woods MAX aerosol, the latest in OFF! Deep Woods, designed for extended protection. On 2nd April 2026, the company said the picaridin-based formula repels mosquitoes and ticks for up to 10 hours.The product is fragrance-free and non-greasy, and safe to use around common outdoor equipment, including fishing lines, sporting gear, and painted surfaces.The launch aligns with a sponsorship making OFF! the official mosquito and tick repellent of Major League Fishing for 2026. The deal includes a brand presence at several MLF events and a sponsored angler team competing in the four 2026 MLF Team Series events.SC Johnson aims to leverage fishing's popularity to promote OFF! Deep Woods MAX and expects increased brand awareness and sales among outdoor enthusiasts who prioritize effective protection.As the outdoor activity market grows, the new repellent addresses a market need and reinforces SC Johnson's commitment to innovative solutions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE [3 News That Matters]

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) SETS UP TOTAL PERIODONTAL HEALTH LAB IN HONG KONG WITH HK DENTAL ASSOCIATION

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) Sets Up TOTAL Periodontal Health Lab in Hong Kong With the Hong Kong Dental Association to promote gum health and preventive care at a community scale. The eight-day event runs from 1st May 2026 to 7th May 2026 at Metro City Plaza in Tseung Kwan O and includes Hong Kong's first community-wide oral health assessment, supported by PARKnSHOP and Watsons under the AS Watson Group. Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) will work with NGOs to offer interactive activities and professional consultations, with dentists and pharmacists providing assessments. The campaign emphasizes daily oral hygiene as a preventive measure against gum disease. Through the Bright Smiles, Bright Futures program, a Smile Power Station will collect smiles for charity, enabling toothpaste donations to the Hong Kong Dental Association's outreach programs for each smile recorded

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) POSTS PROGRESS TOWARD 2030 SUSTAINABILITY & SOCIAL IMPACT STRATEGY

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) Posts Progress Toward 2030 Sustainability & Social Impact Strategy, unveiled Apr. 28, 2026, as a five-year roadmap designed to strengthen resilience and create value for people, pets, and the planet. The plan builds on the 2025 strategy, highlighting milestones such as the 2019 introduction of the first recyclable toothpaste tube, and current packaging progress of 97% of toothpaste SKUs in North America and 98% globally in recyclable tubes, with a target of 100% by 2030. The Bright Smiles, Bright Futures program has reached nearly two billion children and families across more than 100 countries since 1991.The 2030 Strategy centers on three pillars: enhancing social impact, improving hygiene habits, and preserving the environment. Colgate-Palmolive aims to establish a climate-resilient value chain, protect forests and biodiversity, and eliminate plastic waste from its product lineup, while ensuring water security in communities and promoting lasting hygiene habits through various initiatives. It also covers pet care as part of a broader commitment to healthier futures for pets and owners.Ann Tracy, Chief Sustainability Officer, notes the strategy is value-driven and science-based, with new targets to be announced later in 2026. She adds that she posted on LinkedIn that the company seeks to drive value creation that fosters long-term growth and resilience for people, pets, and the planet under its "win-win-win" principle.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) SETS UP SYNDIGO AND FLYWHEEL COLLABORATION TO STREAMLINE PRODUCT DATA ACROSS RETAILERS

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) is expanding its ecommerce data strategy by deploying Syndigo and Flywheel to standardize product information across the retailer network. As of 27th April 2026, the company has begun using Syndigo to distribute product content, including images and detailed descriptions, across a broad set of retailers.The AI-powered Syndigo platform simplifies data exchanges by reducing the need to interface with disparate retailer systems, boosting operational efficiency.Colgate-Palmolive reports a 13% increase in shopper engagement and conversion rates since the transition.Flywheel is handling the workload and centralizing product listings across major retailers, including Kroger, which ranks sixth in the Top 2000 Database of North America’s largest online retailers.With Flywheel, Colgate-Palmolive has already deployed over 220 products through Kroger’s API, enabling faster updates to imagery and descriptions.This strategy underscores a shift toward synchronized product data across digital shelves and retailer ecosystems

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PROCTER AND GAMBLE [3 News That Matters]

+ THE PROCTER & GAMBLE COMPANY (NYSE:PG) SETS BENCHMARK IN HOUSEHOLD PRODUCTS AS FORTUNE NAMES 2026 AMERICA'S MOST INNOVATIVE COMPANIES

The Procter & Gamble Company (NYSE:PG) Posts the top spot in the Household Products Industry on Fortune's 2026 America's Most Innovative Companies list, a distinction it has held for three consecutive years. The announcement was made on 23rd April 2026, and the Fortune ranking, produced in collaboration with Statista, assesses 300 leading U.S. innovators across Product Innovation, Process Innovation and Innovation Culture, with a combined revenue exceeding $12.5 trillion. The ranking reinforces Procter & Gamble's ongoing investments in product development and process improvements that create value for consumers, spanning Fabric Care, Home Care and Personal Health Care. Victor Aguilar, Chief Research, Development & Innovation Officer, said the recognition highlights the company's global reach and its commitment to pushing the boundaries of what's possible, transforming how we innovate. The company continues to leverage a robust intellectual property portfolio to translate scientific advances into everyday products. Tide, Gillette and Olay anchor a diverse brand lineup that serves consumers in approximately 70 countries. For more information, visit https://www.pg.com

+ MR. CLEAN PRO DEBUTS AS P&G PRO’S COMMERCIAL CLEANING SOLUTION

CINCINNATI—Mr. Clean is back in the professional cleaning arena with a new lineup, Mr. Clean PRO, introduced by Procter & Gamble PRO. The program positions the brand as the dependable, next-generation option for business cleaning needs. Today, 10th April 2026, P&G PRO unveiled the commercial-focused range and its plan to help businesses clean more efficiently.Cleaning is a critical operation across restaurants, retail and offices, and Mr. Clean PRO aims to address the persistent gaps in current surface cleaning solutions with commercial-grade formulas designed for grease, stains and grime.Bryan Smith, Vice President North America at P&G PRO, said, 'With Mr. Clean PRO, we’re helping to address those areas of need because choosing the right surface and floor cleaner can make a big difference.' He noted growing demand for solutions that let businesses maintain high cleanliness and efficiency.The line includes products like the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial All Surface Cleaner and the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial Deep Cleaner with Bleach. These products are engineered to deliver the same dependability relied on in households, but with enhanced performance for commercial environments.Mr. Clean PRO products are available nationwide through distributors and retailers, in sizes from 1 gallon to 32 oz. For more information, visit pgpro.com and follow P&G PRO on social media for updates on future innovations and offerings.

+ P&G BROADENS BRAND ENGAGEMENT WITH WNBA ALLIANCE

Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) announced a multi-year, multi-brand partnership with the WNBA designed to deepen brand relevance and broaden reach, integrating flagship brands into athlete collaborations, league events, and digital storytelling.The partnership was revealed on the 9th of April, 2026, and aims to deepen engagement with professional women’s basketball and its rapidly growing fan base by focusing on emotional connections rather than solely on shelf placement, aligning with trends in sports marketing and digital media.P&G intends to connect with younger and more diverse consumers, positioning its legacy brands favorably as the WNBA gains visibility.Investors will assess the campaigns’ effectiveness in terms of brand equity and customer loyalty, with marketing spend in sports and entertainment likely influenced by execution and scale.However, some concerns about P&G’s financial health persist, as shares trade about 13% below analyst expectations and recently fell 6.6% in the past month, underscoring the need for clear disclosures on the returns from the partnership in forthcoming earnings.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHB-19T3-RVM1-00W7-00000-00The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:09ZLexisNexisWEB EXTRASThe Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?Juanpablo RamirezFranco for Grist

The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?

There are no wind turbines on the Great Lakes and it isn't for lack of wind.

This titanic network of interconnected freshwater lakes with a surface area larger than New England, New York, and New Jersey combined is theoretically ideal for wind farms. Winds sweeping across the lakes are stronger, more consistent, and less turbulent than those over land. The National Laboratory of the Rockies has estimated that the Great Lakes states have enough offshore wind potential to generate more than three times their combined annual electricity consumption.

Plus, unlike proposed wind farms along the East Coast, where the federal government controls the seabed, Great Lakes states have jurisdiction over their lakebeds, Grist reports. While projects would still face federal oversight, some proponents see wind development in the Great Lakes as a potential workaround to President Trump's attacks on the industry.

"If it's done correctly and we're able to harness even a fraction of that, we could offset a lot of electricity demand," said Melissa Scanlan, director of the Center for Water Policy at the University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee.

Still, the vast inland lakes remain untapped largely due to a lack of streamlined permitting processes at the state level and economic hurdles. Offshore wind is expensive, and unlike the East Coast, where a mature wind industry has built specialized ships and ports to build turbines in the sea, the Great Lakes states lack the infrastructure. Ultimately, renewable energy experts say the region doesn't have the legal framework or technical capacity to support such massive projects.

So far, offshore wind power which involves installing turbines in large bodies of water, typically the ocean in the United States, has been concentrated along the Eastern seaboard. Projects are still very rare: Just three are currently operating, with several more under construction, all along the East Coast.

More than a decade ago, President Barack Obama's push for renewable energy buoyed a short-lived boom in offshore wind projects particularly in the Great Lakes. The New York Power Authority and cities such as Toronto explored boosting their energy supply with wind power from Lake Ontario and Lake Erie. However, the initial boost only propelled one project forward: Ohio's Icebreaker Wind in Lake Erie. After clashing with Ohio regulators over whether turbines could run through the night and battling a court case filed by local residents over impacts to bats and migratory birds, the developers eventually ran out of money and called the project off in 2023.

Building renewable energy offshore in federal waters has now become harder than ever. On his first day back in office, President Donald Trump signed a memorandum shutting down all offshore wind permitting, effectively suspending five large-scale East Coast projects. Although courts later overturned those cancellations, the uncertainty around offshore wind development under the Trump administration has persisted. And since any project on the Great Lakes would still need federal permits and reviews under the Clean Air Act and the Endangered Species Act, among others, wind developers have largely backed off their plans.

"That told us that this was not the time to reintroduce floating offshore wind in Illinois," said Jim Lanard, co-founder of Magellan Wind, an offshore wind developer focused on the Great Lakes. "Rather, we needed to face the existential threat to the industry."

Lanard said the regulatory uncertainty means it's a bad time for business. His company, which in the past has submitted comments to the Illinois power regulators related to the deployment of offshore wind on Lake Michigan, has temporarily paused pursuing work on the Great Lakes.

"Governors and legislatures are so focused on trying to figure out how to run their governments with the reduced support coming from WashingtonD.C.," Lanard said. "It's not time for them to add additional burdens onto their staff, who don't know how they're going to balance their budgets."

Though Illinois' jurisdiction over part of Lake Michigan presents some opportunities to work around Trump, Lanard said developers are not going to come to Illinois until there is a federal policy to protect the industry from whiplash elections. Moreover, Illinois and the other Great Lakes states have yet to establish a substantial legal framework to make local offshore wind farms a reality. Given these challenges, he estimated it will be some time before there are any wind farms on the Great Lakes.

"I'm looking at five to seven years from 2029," Lanard said.

For years, state Representative Marcus Evans Jr. has championed a plan to make Illinois' shores home to the first offshore wind farm in the Great Lakes. The legislation, known as the Illinois Rust Belt to Green Belt Pilot Program Act, was designed to direct state planners to solicit proposals for a new utility-scale offshore wind farm and begin purchasing offshore wind power. First introduced by Evans in 2022, the measure has long failed to pick up enough momentum to break out of the statehouse. In 2025, Illinois Senator Robert Peters refiled the bill, but it didn't receive a committee assignment. Evans said he decided against refiling the bill this year.

"I didn't have the support," said Evans, a South Side Chicago Democrat. "I used my energy this year to focus on some other items, but it's still something that I think is important," he added.

Since the bill would require a localized industry to build, transport, and upkeep the turbines, Evans saw the push as a huge boon for the region's clean energy workforce one that the state of Illinois has long been trying to prop up.

Illinois passed the Climate and Equitable Jobs Act in 2021, which committed the state to investing $80 million annually to build out workforce training hubs focused on clean energy-related jobs. The same bill also put the state on track to transition its energy sector to 100 percent clean energy by 2050. Evans had hoped his initiative could help achieve both goals at once.

He is still optimistic that Lake Michigan will be next.

"I will file [a bill] in the future," he said.

This story is a partnership between Grist and WBEZ, a public radio station serving the Chicago metropolitan region.

This story was produced by Grist and reviewed and distributed by Stacker.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK7-7K23-S048-H21D-00000-00SC JOHNSON & SON - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:09ZLexisNexisSTOCKSC JOHNSON & SON - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: SC JOHNSON &SON

PermID4298009420Websitehttp://www.scjohnson.com/en/home.aspx?directLink=yIndustryManufacturer of household products

ACTIVITIES:

SC Johnson & Son, founded in 1886 in Racine, Wisconsin, is a family-owned consumer goods company. With $11 billion in revenue and 13,000 employees, it manufactures household brands like Windex, Glade, and Ziploc, operating in 70 countries. SC Johnson emphasizes sustainability, using renewable energy and reducing plastic waste. Its innovation center develops eco-friendly products, and its transparency in ingredient disclosure sets industry standards. Led by H. Fisk Johnson, the companys philanthropy includes land conservation and education grants. SC Johnsons commitment to quality and environmental responsibility reinforces its global leadership in household goods.

+ SC JOHNSON & SON [3 News That Matters]

+ SC JOHNSON UNVEILS OUTDOOR OFF! AND RAID PRODUCTS, SIGNALS SPACE BAG AND BABYGANICS ACQUISITIONS

SC Johnson & Son is expanding its outdoor portfolio with a new round of products under OFF! and Raid geared toward spring and summer activities. This announcement was made on 7th May 2026, as the company positions its brands for an active outdoor season.The OFF! line includes formulations designed to control mosquitoes, ants, and wasps, with prices starting at $5, appealing to a broad range of backyard users.In a separate strategic move, SC Johnson disclosed an agreement to acquire the global Space Bag brand from ITW, expected to finalize in coming months, pending U.S. regulatory approvals. Financial terms were not disclosed.The company has also acquired Babyganics, a rapidly growing personal care brand founded in 2004, offering skin care, bath care, insect repellent, and cleaning products, expanding its footprint in the family and personal care market.These moves position SC Johnson to capitalize on demand for outdoor products and family care solutions.

+ YOUTH LEADERS TAKE CENTER STAGE AS SC JOHNSON PARTNERS WITH EARTHECHO TO COMBAT PLASTIC WASTE

SC Johnson & Son and EarthEcho International announced on March 31, 2026 that the Marine Plastics Ambassadors program will expand into its largest cohort yet, the latest phase in their three-year partnership to curb plastic waste.The program will provide participants with advocacy training and opportunities for policy engagement, along with mentorship to help them design scalable solutions for their local communities."The Marine Plastics Ambassadors are leading the way, and EarthEcho is proud to build a community of practice that empowers them to change the world," said Philippe Cousteau, Jr., founder of EarthEcho International."It's encouraging to see young people stepping forward to help tackle this important issue. As a company, we advocate for smart government regulation as a crucial tool in tackling the plastic waste crisis. We're pleased to partner with EarthEcho to support this next generation of leaders in their advocacy efforts," said Fisk Johnson, Chairman and CEO of SC Johnson.Participants will convene in Washington, D.C., this summer to advance policy priorities and engage with legislators, demonstrating the effectiveness of youth-driven solutions. With more than 430 million tons of plastic produced globally each year, and projections that plastic entering oceans could nearly triple by 2040, the partnership underscores the urgency of addressing plastic waste. For more information on the Marine Plastics Ambassadors and their initiatives, visit EarthEcho's official website.

+ OFF! DEEP WOODS MAX AEROSOL DEBUTS AS A 10-HOUR PICARIDIN REPELLENT

SC Johnson and Son introduced Deep Woods MAX aerosol, the latest in OFF! Deep Woods, designed for extended protection. On 2nd April 2026, the company said the picaridin-based formula repels mosquitoes and ticks for up to 10 hours.The product is fragrance-free and non-greasy, and safe to use around common outdoor equipment, including fishing lines, sporting gear, and painted surfaces.The launch aligns with a sponsorship making OFF! the official mosquito and tick repellent of Major League Fishing for 2026. The deal includes a brand presence at several MLF events and a sponsored angler team competing in the four 2026 MLF Team Series events.SC Johnson aims to leverage fishing's popularity to promote OFF! Deep Woods MAX and expects increased brand awareness and sales among outdoor enthusiasts who prioritize effective protection.As the outdoor activity market grows, the new repellent addresses a market need and reinforces SC Johnson's commitment to innovative solutions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE [3 News That Matters]

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) APPOINTS NICOLAS TRAN DINH APAC DIGITAL & MEDIA DIRECTOR

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) Appoints Nicolas Tran Dinh APAC Digital & Media Director. Dinh will lead full-funnel media strategies, geographic operations, and digital transformation initiatives across the Asia-Pacific region to strengthen Colgate-Palmolive's digital footprint in one of the fastest-growing marketsThe company said on 13th May 2026 that the appointment aligns with its effort to adapt marketing and media strategies to evolving consumer expectations in the digital age. Dinh will prioritize data analytics and innovative digital solutions to boost brand engagement and drive growth across diverse APAC marketsDinh brings extensive experience in digital marketing and media management, with leadership roles in multinational corporations. His background in digital transformation will guide Colgate-Palmolive through the shifting advertising landscape and changing consumer behaviorColgate-Palmolive's decision to elevate Dinh underscores a strategic priority to integrate digital technologies into core operations and optimize marketing spend while ensuring that digital initiatives resonate with the target audience. As digital advertising evolves, having a dedicated APAC leader signals Colgate-Palmolive's ongoing effort to maintain its competitive edgeObservers will watch closely how Dinh's leadership shapes Colgate-Palmolive's brand presence in APAC amid evolving opportunities and challenges in the digital marketplace

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) DEBUTS EVERY COLGATE SMILE HAS A STORY ACROSS INDIA CAMPAIGN

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) announced on 6th May 2026 the launch of Every Colgate Smile Has A Story by Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited, a storytelling campaign marking the brand's 90-year legacy in India. The initiative highlights the enduring bond between Colgate and Indian households, with the iconic Colgate toothpaste tube long viewed by mothers as a trusted member of the family.Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited designed the campaign to celebrate the trust mothers place in Colgate and to portray the universal aim of protecting families. The first film Raised by Sweets follows Vertica Khandelwal’s Dehradun family, who have created sweets for 125 years, as she navigates sharing treats with her children while relying on Colgate’s protective benefits. The series explores real stories and the impact of Colgate products on daily life.The Arginine Calcium Boost Technology provides 24-hour cavity protection, reinforcing the campaigns message.Gunjit Jain, Executive Vice President Marketing at Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited, says the project goes beyond traditional advertising to reinforce the trust that mothers have placed in Colgate for decades. Colgate-Palmolive plans to amplify Every Colgate Smile Has A Story across television, digital platforms and social channels while advancing sustainability efforts through reduced plastic waste and broader oral health education via the Bright Smiles Bright Futures program.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) EXPANDS 90-YEAR INDIA CAMPAIGN WITH FOCUS ON TRUST AND FAMILY STORIES

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) marks a milestone in India, celebrating 90 years as a trusted household name through the 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' campaign, launched on the 6th of May, 2026. The initiative underscores the emotional connections families have with the brand and reinforces Colgate's leadership in oral health solutions.The campaign features real-life narratives, including Vertica Khandelwal from Dehradun, who balances cultural heritage with dental health, illustrating how Colgate provides reassurance with its slogan 'Colgate Sambhal Lega'.Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited continues to lead the Indian oral care market by combining emotional resonance with scientific integrity. The brand's Arginine + Calcium Boost Technology delivers 24-hour cavity protection, reflecting the company's ongoing commitment to innovation.The campaign also reflects Colgate-Palmolive's understanding of trust in consumer relationships. By connecting with families through relatable stories, the brand reinforces its promise of protection and care, a cornerstone of its identity in the Indian market for nearly a century.As Colgate-Palmolive evolves its marketing strategies, the 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' campaign serves as a reminder of the brand's enduring impact on Indian households, aiming to strengthen its legacy and remain a trusted partner in oral health for generations to come.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PROCTER AND GAMBLE [3 News That Matters]

+ THE PROCTER & GAMBLE COMPANY (NYSE:PG) SETS BENCHMARK IN HOUSEHOLD PRODUCTS AS FORTUNE NAMES 2026 AMERICA'S MOST INNOVATIVE COMPANIES

The Procter & Gamble Company (NYSE:PG) Posts the top spot in the Household Products Industry on Fortune's 2026 America's Most Innovative Companies list, a distinction it has held for three consecutive years. The announcement was made on 23rd April 2026, and the Fortune ranking, produced in collaboration with Statista, assesses 300 leading U.S. innovators across Product Innovation, Process Innovation and Innovation Culture, with a combined revenue exceeding $12.5 trillion. The ranking reinforces Procter & Gamble's ongoing investments in product development and process improvements that create value for consumers, spanning Fabric Care, Home Care and Personal Health Care. Victor Aguilar, Chief Research, Development & Innovation Officer, said the recognition highlights the company's global reach and its commitment to pushing the boundaries of what's possible, transforming how we innovate. The company continues to leverage a robust intellectual property portfolio to translate scientific advances into everyday products. Tide, Gillette and Olay anchor a diverse brand lineup that serves consumers in approximately 70 countries. For more information, visit https://www.pg.com

+ MR. CLEAN PRO DEBUTS AS P&G PRO’S COMMERCIAL CLEANING SOLUTION

CINCINNATI—Mr. Clean is back in the professional cleaning arena with a new lineup, Mr. Clean PRO, introduced by Procter & Gamble PRO. The program positions the brand as the dependable, next-generation option for business cleaning needs. Today, 10th April 2026, P&G PRO unveiled the commercial-focused range and its plan to help businesses clean more efficiently.Cleaning is a critical operation across restaurants, retail and offices, and Mr. Clean PRO aims to address the persistent gaps in current surface cleaning solutions with commercial-grade formulas designed for grease, stains and grime.Bryan Smith, Vice President North America at P&G PRO, said, 'With Mr. Clean PRO, we’re helping to address those areas of need because choosing the right surface and floor cleaner can make a big difference.' He noted growing demand for solutions that let businesses maintain high cleanliness and efficiency.The line includes products like the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial All Surface Cleaner and the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial Deep Cleaner with Bleach. These products are engineered to deliver the same dependability relied on in households, but with enhanced performance for commercial environments.Mr. Clean PRO products are available nationwide through distributors and retailers, in sizes from 1 gallon to 32 oz. For more information, visit pgpro.com and follow P&G PRO on social media for updates on future innovations and offerings.

+ P&G BROADENS BRAND ENGAGEMENT WITH WNBA ALLIANCE

Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) announced a multi-year, multi-brand partnership with the WNBA designed to deepen brand relevance and broaden reach, integrating flagship brands into athlete collaborations, league events, and digital storytelling.The partnership was revealed on the 9th of April, 2026, and aims to deepen engagement with professional women’s basketball and its rapidly growing fan base by focusing on emotional connections rather than solely on shelf placement, aligning with trends in sports marketing and digital media.P&G intends to connect with younger and more diverse consumers, positioning its legacy brands favorably as the WNBA gains visibility.Investors will assess the campaigns’ effectiveness in terms of brand equity and customer loyalty, with marketing spend in sports and entertainment likely influenced by execution and scale.However, some concerns about P&G’s financial health persist, as shares trade about 13% below analyst expectations and recently fell 6.6% in the past month, underscoring the need for clear disclosures on the returns from the partnership in forthcoming earnings.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RB [3 News That Matters]

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP PLC (L:RKT) SETS OUT DIGITAL STRATEGY AMID CHINA E-COMMERCE SURGE NEAR £1 BILLION REVENUE

Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC (L:RKT) is navigating a pivotal moment as it adapts to China's fast-evolving e-commerce landscape. Kris Licht, CEO of Reckitt, describes the transition as the most profound channel shift he has encountered. As of 9th May 2026, e-commerce and social commerce combined account for roughly 80% of China sales, up from 30% six years ago, and the region's revenue is nearing £1 billion after 11 consecutive quarters of double-digit growth. The shift aligns with Reckitt's broader push toward digital sales initiatives, accelerated product cycles, and the integration of artificial intelligence. The Focus On: Digital Science virtual investor event scheduled for 14th May will illustrate how its digital and R&D teams are leveraging technology to foster innovation and drive category growth in China. The company also disclosed a buyback of 190,500 ordinary shares totaling about £9 million as part of capital returns. However, management warned that first-quarter revenue missed expectations and forecast margins to dip in the first half. Analysts from Aviva Investors and JPMorgan described the quarter as broadly muted in growth and questioned the ability to meet full-year targets, particularly in emerging markets. The external environment remains challenging, with rising costs and price pressures weighing on margins. Unilever has signaled modest price increases; Reckitt has indicated margins could come under pressure amid higher input costs and a shift toward lower-priced private-label options. The sector's shift toward cheaper alternatives presents an additional hurdle for branded health and hygiene products. Zavier Wong of eToro noted that firms hedging against these pressures are better positioned to weather them. Russia sanctions add further complexity, with Reckitt's local arm pursuing new product development and IP filings to fill gaps left by the sanctions. Licht remains cautiously optimistic that accelerated digital moves could offset some drag, but investors will want to see tangible sales gains beyond China as the company executes its broader digital strategy.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP PLC (L:RKT) SETS COURSE FOR AIR FRESHENERS GROWTH AS MARKET FORECAST REACHES $19.75 BILLION BY 2030

Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC (L:RKT) and peers in the air fresheners sector pursue a market forecast calling for growth to $19.75 billion by 2030. Global demand remains supported by urbanization and ongoing fragrance technology innovation. As of 27th April 2026, the market is set to rise from $14.36 billion in 2025 to $15.43 billion in 2026, reflecting a 7.5% compound annual growth rate.RB and peers such as Godrej Consumer Products and Procter & Gamble actively pursue new fragrance solutions to capture expanding interest in home ambiance and wellness. The push to improve indoor air quality and lifestyle quality drives demand for air fresheners, aided by higher household spending and broader retail access.Industry dynamics show Europe leading in 2025, with Asia-Pacific projected to be the fastest growing region in the coming years. Companies reinforce growth through sustainable delivery technologies, including Everspray micro-diffusion aerosol systems that deliver ultra-fine droplets with reduced waste, while other innovations minimize environmental impact.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP PLC (L:RKT) HARRY KIRSCH JOINS GALDERMA BOARD, STRENGTHENING GOVERNANCE LINK

Reckitt Benckiser Group plc (L:RKT) has appointed Harry Kirsch, a non-executive director, to the board of Galderma (SIX:GALD) with effect from 28th April 2026. Galderma, a Swiss-based company focused on dermatology products, will gain Kirsch's governance experience.The appointment was disclosed under the Financial Conduct Authority's Listing Rule 9.6.14 in a Reckitt Benckiser press release. Kirsch will continue to serve as a non-executive director at Reckitt, and terms of the Galderma appointment were not disclosed.The move could foster synergies in product development and market expansion, linking Reckitt's consumer health brands with Galderma's dermatology portfolio. Investors will monitor Galderma's governance and strategic direction as Kirsch contributes to its board.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) AND MCCORMICK & CO. (NYSE:MKC) STRIKE $44.8 BILLION MERGER TO EXPAND SEASONINGS EMPIRE

Unilever PLC (L:ULVR) and McCormick & Co. (NYSE:MKC) have agreed to merge their food operations in a transaction valued at $44.8 billion, combining $15.7 billion in cash with about $29.1 billion in McCormick stock for a substantial portion of Unilever's food business. Unilever and its shareholders will own 65% of the new entity, while McCormick takes the remaining stake. The merged group will include iconic brands such as French's mustard and Hellmann's mayonnaise. The parties disclosed the deal on 11th May 2026. The move aims to bolster McCormick’s packaged foods footprint and help Unilever sharpen its focus on beauty, personal care and home care. Market reaction was cautious as shares moved lower: McCormick's stock fell as much as 10% in U.S. trading and closed down 21% year-to-date, while Unilever’s London-listed shares declined 7.3%, contributing to a roughly 14% year-to-date drop. The transaction stands as the largest in the histories of both companies, underscoring a major rebalancing of their strategies

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) ESG COMMITMENTS IN FOCUS AS MCCORMICK MERGER PROGRESSES

Unilever and McCormick plan to merge their food businesses in a $65 billion deal disclosed in March, creating a global platform that includes Hellmann's and Cholula. The arrangement, highlighted on 8th May 2026, would boost McCormick's scale and bring a more complex supply chain tied to farming and smallholders. Investors call for binding sustainability protections. Storebrand's Vemund Olsen, a senior analyst, argues the merged company should maintain deforestation-free sourcing and full traceability. Storebrand is among the top holders of both Unilever and McCormick shares, underscoring the focus on ESG amid the merger. Regulatory regimes differ between the EU and the US, complicating disclosures. McCormick is not bound by Europe’s stricter rules, while Unilever remains under them; transition rules could rely on voluntary commitments for a period. As You Sow's Cailin Dendas warns that if sustainability does not receive priority, it could pose significant risks for shareholders and the merged group. Governance and commitments. Unilever is expected to retain about 10% ownership and four board seats, preserving influence though smaller investors may have limited say in governance. A Unilever spokesperson says the company is actively working with McCormick to align food-related sustainability programs as the merger progresses. McCormick's sustainability profile remains a focal point. Sustainalytics analyst Hannah Schalk notes a medium-risk exposure and a lack of a formal no-deforestation pledge, with limited details on traceability and auditing. McCormick acknowledges that achieving emissions and sourcing targets will rely on stronger supplier engagement and better data across its supply chain. The firm adds that it is pursuing a comprehensive strategic update process for its sustainability program, while declining to comment on future targets at this time.

+ UNILEVER PLC (LSE:ULVR) SIGNALS POTENTIAL SPIN-OFF OF FOOD ARM AS FOCUS SHIFTS TO BEAUTY AND WELLNESS

Unilever PLC (LSE:ULVR) is weighing the separation of its food assets as part of a broader shift toward growing its beauty, personal care and wellbeing brands. On 29th April 2026, advisers are being engaged to assess options, including a potential divestment of the food business that includes Hellmann's and Knorr. The discussions are in early stages, with scenarios ranging from a full spin-off to retaining flagship brands while divesting others, and a formal decision is not expected before 2027. Industry experts estimate a tens-of-billions-of-dollars transaction if a sale proceeds, though no final course has been chosen. The food division, which also covers Colman's and Marmite, has seen shares drift about 0.7% lower, keeping Unilever's market cap around £107 billion ($142 billion). Under Fernandez, the company is sharpening its focus on beauty brands like Dove and products such as Liquid IV, having spun off the ice cream unit last year while retaining a minority stake to be sold gradually. Barclays' Warren Ackerman says the strategic direction is evident, though market conditions may affect the timing of any food separation. Unilever has previously disposed of its global spreads division and several snack brands, leaving roughly €1 billion to €1.5 billion of local food brands still to exit. The move could recalibrate the company's investment trajectory amid a broader shift toward value and private-label offerings in the food sector
